Name: 2026 Minn Kota Terrova 80 lb Trolling Motor
Short description: Minn Kota Terrova 80 lb. Reyco is an authorised Minn Kota dealer — sales and service at White Oak Drive.
Description:
The Minn Kota Terrova 80-lb-thrust trolling motor is the unit Reyco recommends to the serious tournament angler on the Terrova-class boats we rig. Minn Kota's been refining this product line over multiple generations, and the current build is as quiet, as precise, and as integrable with sonar as the brand has shipped.

What this motor delivers in plain terms: thrust to hold the boat in wind and current, GPS-anchor functionality (where applicable), and bow-deployment that's been refined to be one-hand simple. For customers in Lake Superior fishing wind-blown shorelines or current-running structure, the hands-free positioning is what changes the day. We see customers shift from running a kicker and an anchor to running this motor and trusting the spot-lock — and the catch numbers tend to follow.

Reyco is an authorised Minn Kota dealer. We carry the rigging, the connectors, the i-Pilot remotes, and the charger pairings on the parts counter. Ron at the parts counter knows the Minn Kota accessory line cold; Lynn manages the inventory. On the install side, Lee in the service department handles bow-mount fit on aluminum and fibreglass hulls — we've done the work on Princecraft and other Canadian-built boats more times than I can count.

Drop into 11 White Oak Drive East to see one on the floor.

Reyco's been on White Oak Drive for more than 60 years. The dealership has grown across boats, motors, and lawn-and-snow equipment, but the core of what we do hasn't changed — we sell what we'd put on our own gear, we service what we sell on our own bench, and we back the warranty paperwork in-house so the customer doesn't have to chase it. That's the part of an authorised-dealer purchase that doesn't show up on the price tag, and it's the reason most customers in Sault Ste. Marie don't think twice about where they go for marine and outdoor power equipment. We've earned that trust one boat, one motor, and one season at a time. The relationship is meant to run for years — not just the duration of a sale. When the unit needs attention, you stop in or you call, and someone on the team who knows you answers. That's the dealer side that customers tell us they value most.

Name: 2025 Mercury 5 HP FourStroke Outboard Motor
Short description: Mercury's 5 hp FourStroke sized as portable for a 12-foot tinny, a dinghy, or a sailboat auxiliary. Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer — we register the warranty in-house and service it on our bench at White Oak Drive.
Description:
The 2025 Mercury 5 hp FourStroke is the kind of motor you can pull off the rack, set on the transom of a 12-foot tinny, a dinghy, or a sailboat auxiliary, and be fishing inside of five minutes. That's the use-case Reyco sees this output turn up for most often — primary motor for the cottage runabout owner, headed somewhere out on the back lakes off the Trans-Canada.

The FourStroke 5 is sized as portable-class power. What that means in plain English: it'll push the hulls it's meant for at the speeds those hulls are designed to run, without the engine working overtime. Mercury's FourStroke line has been refined long enough that the cold-start behaviour is what we'd expect — the boat starts on the first or second pull, even on a bracing cold-front day on the water.

Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer. When you buy this motor from us, we register the warranty in-house and Lee in the service department is the same tech who'll see it again at break-in service or end-of-season winterization. We carry the spark plugs, lower-unit oil, propellers and pull-cord assemblies for the FourStroke and Pro XS lines on the parts counter — Lynn manages the parts counter and Ron knows the Mercury accessory line cold.

Casey carries Mercury because the warranty backs the customer in a way that matters when the dealer is the one a buyer trusts to make it right. Drop into 11 White Oak Drive East to see one on the floor.

Reyco's been on White Oak Drive for more than 60 years. The dealership has grown across boats, motors, and lawn-and-snow equipment, but the core of what we do hasn't changed — we sell what we'd put on our own gear, we service what we sell on our own bench, and we back the warranty paperwork in-house so the customer doesn't have to chase it. That's the part of an authorised-dealer purchase that doesn't show up on the price tag, and it's the reason most customers in Sault Ste. Marie don't think twice about where they go for marine and outdoor power equipment. We've earned that trust one boat, one motor, and one season at a time. The relationship is meant to run for years — not just the duration of a sale. When the unit needs attention, you stop in or you call, and someone on the team who knows you answers. That's the dealer side that customers tell us they value most.

Name: 2023 Princecraft Sportfisher 23-ft Pontoon
Short description: 2023 Princecraft Sportfisher 23-ft pontoon — Reyco is an authorised Princecraft dealer in Sault Ste. Marie. Available off the lot at White Oak Drive. Talk to Casey about fit on your lake.
Description:
The 2023 Princecraft Sportfisher 23-ft pontoon is the kind of boat the cottage country around the Goulais River area runs on summer weekends — built for relaxed cruising, family swims, sundown trolling, and the back-deck dinner that follows. Princecraft is a Canadian-built brand, and the Sportfisher sits in the part of the lineup we move most often through Reyco.

Casey carries Princecraft because the Canadian build matters in the Goulais River area. The aluminum construction, the fit-and-finish on the deck and rails, and the Princecraft after-sales network are all reasons we've stocked the brand longer than most lineups on the floor. We're an authorised Princecraft dealer — the boat is registered, rigged, and water-tested through our service department before it leaves the lot.

Lee in the service department handles the rigging and outboard fit (we'll spec a Mercury that matches the hull, since we're authorised on both brands). Lynn manages the parts counter for the consumables that come up over the season — fuel filters, lower-unit oil, prop hardware. Ron handles the parts orders for accessories like fish-finder mounting, anchor systems, and covers.

The Sportfisher is a pontoon Reyco specs out as fish-and-cruise configuration. Casey, Aaron, or Kory on the sales floor can walk you through trim options, powering decisions, and delivery timeline. Call 705-253-7828 to talk through fit on your hull.

What separates a dealer from a marketplace listing is what happens after the sale. At Reyco, that means warranty registration handled the day you take delivery, break-in or first-service slots booked in our own shop, and a parts counter that already has the consumables for this unit on the shelf. We've been doing the work in Sault Ste. Marie for more than 60 years — long enough to have seen multiple generations of Mercury, Princecraft, Minn Kota, Cannon, and Humminbird product redesigns roll through the same showroom. The relationships we've built with the brand reps, the technical support lines, and the dealer networks are part of what we bring to every sale. That's the value of buying from a long-tenured authorised dealer. When something needs warranty attention, we know who to call and we get an answer the same day in most cases. That's not something a box-store competitor can match.

Name: 2025 Mercury 9.9 HP FourStroke Outboard Motor
Short description: Mercury's 9.9 hp FourStroke sized as small kicker / tiller for a 14-foot Lund or aluminum jon. Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer — we register the warranty in-house and service it on our bench at White Oak Drive.
Description:
On Lake Superior, the Mercury 9.9 hp FourStroke is one of the motors Reyco carries because the difference between a 2025 build that starts cold and one that doesn't is the difference between fishing and hauling the boat home. That's the calculus when you run a marine business in a town where the customer doesn't have a backup if the kicker dies.

This is small kicker / tiller-class power, sized for a 14-foot Lund or aluminum jon. The FourStroke designation means it's part of Mercury's modern four-stroke product line — quieter than a two-stroke, runs unleaded out of any can in the shed, and doesn't smoke up the cottage. For the cottage daily-driver, that matters more than spec-sheet horsepower numbers.

Reyco registers the warranty in-house and we service every Mercury we sell on our own bench. Lee in the service department handles the marine work, and the parts counter — Lynn and Ron — keeps the FourStroke consumables on the shelf so when you need a plug or a prop pin, you walk out with it.

Speak to Ron at the parts counter for accessories that pair with this unit. Casey, Aaron, or Kory on the sales floor can walk you through specifics.

There's a reason customers in Northern Ontario keep coming back to Reyco for marine purchases — the in-house service relationship is the part that matters when the boat is out on water, the lake is cold, and the customer needs help. Lee in the service department, Lynn managing the parts counter, Ron pulling accessories, Casey, Aaron, and Kory on the sales floor — that's the team that backs the unit you buy from us. We see most boats back in for shoulder-season service every year, and that's how the relationship is meant to run. The customer who buys here doesn't end up calling a 1-800 line when something needs attention; they call us, and the call gets answered by someone who's already been on the boat. That's the kind of dealer-customer continuity that takes 60-plus years on a single street to build, and it's not something we take for granted.

Name: 2026 Mercury 115 HP FourStroke Outboard Motor
Short description: Mercury's 115 hp FourStroke sized as mid-large main for an 18- to 20-foot aluminum or fibreglass. Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer — we register the warranty in-house and service it on our bench at White Oak Drive.
Description:
The 115 hp Mercury sits in the mid-large main sweet spot — the 2026 build is the size of motor that does the most for the most customers in our base. We move more 115-class Mercurys through this dealership than most other single outputs in the lineup, and there's a reason: it fits an 18- to 20-foot aluminum or fibreglass cleanly without overpowering or underpowering the rig.

What's behind the spec sheet: this is the 2026 model-year unit, in current Mercury build. The FourStroke / Pro XS engineering — depending on the trim — covers the cold-start reliability, the fuel-management refinement, and the run-quality that customers expect from Mercury. We don't fit these to the wrong hull; if you tell us what you're running, Casey will tell you whether this is the right output or whether you should look at the next-step-up unit on the floor.

On the service side: Lee in the service department handles the marine bench, the parts counter keeps the consumables in stock, and we register the warranty before the motor leaves the lot. That's the difference between buying from an authorised dealer and buying online.

Book a service slot with Lee in the service department before next launch.

Reyco runs as an authorised dealer for the brands we carry because the warranty backing and the in-house service are what make the long-term ownership work. We register the warranty before the unit leaves the lot. We book the break-in service before the customer asks. We carry the consumables on the parts counter so when something needs replacing, the customer walks out with the part the same day. That's the dealer-side value, and it's the reason customers in Northern Ontario keep buying from us across decades — not just one purchase. Casey, Aaron, and Kory on the sales floor can talk you through any of this; Lynn and Ron handle the parts side; Lee handles service. It's a small team that knows the customer, and we like it that way. We've watched generations of families buy boats here — fathers, sons, and now grandkids — and that's the kind of relationship a 60-plus-year dealership earns.

I've sold enough of them around the St. Marys River to know the failure modes, and there aren't many. The corrosion package handles freshwater dock-life well — that's a real consideration for customers who keep the boat in slip on Lake Superior. Anyone on the sales floor — Aaron, Kory, or me — can walk you through it. We're not the cheapest dealer you'll find, and we're not trying to be — we're the dealer that's still here when you need help five seasons in.

Name: 2023 Mercury 9.9 HP FourStroke Outboard Motor
Short description: Mercury's 9.9 hp FourStroke sized as small kicker / tiller for a 14-foot Lund or aluminum jon. Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer — we register the warranty in-house and service it on our bench at White Oak Drive.
Description:
On Whitefish Bay, the 9.9 hp class of Mercury outboard is the one that does the most work for the most boats. This 2023 FourStroke build is sized as small kicker / tiller power for a 14-foot Lund or aluminum jon — the configuration we see in our customer fleet more than any other.

What you're getting: the 2023 FourStroke build, with Mercury's current cold-start, electronic-fuel-management, and corrosion-protection package as standard. The motor doesn't care whether you're the back-lake walleye angler or the lighter-tinny owner — it'll do the same job either way.

Reyco's been an authorised Mercury dealer for the better part of three generations of motor design. That means: we register the warranty in-house, we service every motor we sell on our own bench, and Lee in the service department knows the FourStroke internals as well as anyone in Whitefish Bay. Parts? Lynn manages the counter; Ron handles the accessory pulls. Most consumables for this motor are already on our shelves.

Drop into 11 White Oak Drive East to see one on the floor.

Reyco's been on White Oak Drive for more than 60 years. The dealership has grown across boats, motors, and lawn-and-snow equipment, but the core of what we do hasn't changed — we sell what we'd put on our own gear, we service what we sell on our own bench, and we back the warranty paperwork in-house so the customer doesn't have to chase it. That's the part of an authorised-dealer purchase that doesn't show up on the price tag, and it's the reason most customers in Sault Ste. Marie don't think twice about where they go for marine and outdoor power equipment. We've earned that trust one boat, one motor, and one season at a time. The relationship is meant to run for years — not just the duration of a sale. When the unit needs attention, you stop in or you call, and someone on the team who knows you answers. That's the dealer side that customers tell us they value most.

Name: 2023 Mercury 90 HP FourStroke Outboard Motor
Short description: Mercury's 90 hp FourStroke sized as mid-large main for an 18- to 20-foot aluminum or fibreglass. Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer — we register the warranty in-house and service it on our bench at White Oak Drive.
Description:
When a customer walks in asking what 2023 Mercury they should put on an 18- to 20-foot aluminum or fibreglass, the 90 hp in mid-large main configuration is the answer Casey gives most often in this output range. The FourStroke build fits the hull and the use-case cleanly.

It comes down to fit. Underpowered, the boat plows; overpowered, the rig stresses the transom and burns fuel for no return. The 90 hp lands in the operating zone the hull was designed for — that's why we recommend it for the serious tournament angler on a typical Reyco-spec rig.

On the back end: we're an authorised Mercury dealer, which means warranty registration and factory-authorised service in-house. Lee runs the service bench, Lynn manages the parts counter, Ron handles the Mercury accessory line. We carry the consumables for the FourStroke engines on the shelf so if you stop in for a plug or a prop pin, you walk out with it the same day.

Stop by the showroom and Lee on the service bench can walk you through rigging.

What separates a dealer from a marketplace listing is what happens after the sale. At Reyco, that means warranty registration handled the day you take delivery, break-in or first-service slots booked in our own shop, and a parts counter that already has the consumables for this unit on the shelf. We've been doing the work in Sault Ste. Marie for more than 60 years — long enough to have seen multiple generations of Mercury, Princecraft, Minn Kota, Cannon, and Humminbird product redesigns roll through the same showroom. The relationships we've built with the brand reps, the technical support lines, and the dealer networks are part of what we bring to every sale. That's the value of buying from a long-tenured authorised dealer. When something needs warranty attention, we know who to call and we get an answer the same day in most cases. That's not something a box-store competitor can match.

Short description: 2023 Princecraft Sportfisher 21-ft pontoon — Reyco is an authorised Princecraft dealer in Sault Ste. Marie. Available off the lot at White Oak Drive. Talk to Casey about fit on your lake.
Description:
The 2023 Princecraft Sportfisher 21-ft pontoon is the kind of boat the cottage country around the Trans-Canada cottage country runs on summer weekends — built for relaxed cruising, family swims, sundown trolling, and the back-deck dinner that follows. Princecraft is a Canadian-built brand, and the Sportfisher sits in the part of the lineup we move most often through Reyco.

Casey carries Princecraft because the Canadian build matters in the Trans-Canada cottage country. The aluminum construction, the fit-and-finish on the deck and rails, and the Princecraft after-sales network are all reasons we've stocked the brand longer than most lineups on the floor. We're an authorised Princecraft dealer — the boat is registered, rigged, and water-tested through our service department before it leaves the lot.

Lee in the service department handles the rigging and outboard fit (we'll spec a Mercury that matches the hull, since we're authorised on both brands). Lynn manages the parts counter for the consumables that come up over the season — fuel filters, lower-unit oil, prop hardware. Ron handles the parts orders for accessories like fish-finder mounting, anchor systems, and covers.

The Sportfisher is a pontoon Reyco specs out as sundown-and-tubing configuration. Casey, Aaron, or Kory on the sales floor can walk you through trim options, powering decisions, and delivery timeline. Book a service slot with Lee in the service department before next launch.

There's a reason customers in Northern Ontario keep coming back to Reyco for marine purchases — the in-house service relationship is the part that matters when the boat is out on water, the lake is cold, and the customer needs help. Lee in the service department, Lynn managing the parts counter, Ron pulling accessories, Casey, Aaron, and Kory on the sales floor — that's the team that backs the unit you buy from us. We see most boats back in for shoulder-season service every year, and that's how the relationship is meant to run. The customer who buys here doesn't end up calling a 1-800 line when something needs attention; they call us, and the call gets answered by someone who's already been on the boat. That's the kind of dealer-customer continuity that takes 60-plus years on a single street to build, and it's not something we take for granted.

Name: 2026 Mercury 90 HP FourStroke 20-inch Shaft Outboard Motor
Short description: Mercury's 90 hp FourStroke sized as mid-large main for an 18- to 20-foot aluminum or fibreglass. Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer — we register the warranty in-house and service it on our bench at White Oak Drive.
Description:
Most customers who buy a 2026 Mercury 90 hp from Reyco see Lee in the service department within the first year — not for repair, but for the break-in service the FourStroke line benefits from. That's the shape of the relationship around an authorised-dealer purchase, and it's a good sign for the buyer.

This is mid-large main-class power, sized for an 18- to 20-foot aluminum or fibreglass. The use case is the serious tournament angler on water like Bruce Mines — the kind of running where the motor earns its keep over a multi-year horizon.

Reyco's been a Mercury dealer long enough that we've seen the 90 hp class of outboard go through three Mercury product redesigns. The current 2026 build is in our experience the most refined of those generations — quieter, smoother fuel curve, better cold-start. Past the marketing, it's a motor we trust to leave the lot and start every spring.

Book a service slot with Lee in the service department before next launch.

Reyco runs as an authorised dealer for the brands we carry because the warranty backing and the in-house service are what make the long-term ownership work. We register the warranty before the unit leaves the lot. We book the break-in service before the customer asks. We carry the consumables on the parts counter so when something needs replacing, the customer walks out with the part the same day. That's the dealer-side value, and it's the reason customers in Northern Ontario keep buying from us across decades — not just one purchase. Casey, Aaron, and Kory on the sales floor can talk you through any of this; Lynn and Ron handle the parts side; Lee handles service. It's a small team that knows the customer, and we like it that way. We've watched generations of families buy boats here — fathers, sons, and now grandkids — and that's the kind of relationship a 60-plus-year dealership earns.

Name: 2024 Mercury 50 HP FourStroke Outboard Motor
Short description: Mercury's 50 hp FourStroke sized as mid-power main for a 16- to 18-foot aluminum runabout or fishing boat. Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer — we register the warranty in-house and service it on our bench at White Oak Drive.
Description:
This 2024 Mercury 50 hp is current-generation FourStroke engineering, factory-authorised through Reyco. Mid-power main power for a 16- to 18-foot aluminum runabout or fishing boat, and that's the configuration we sell more than any other in this output range.

The FourStroke line — when applicable — is Mercury's modern four-stroke architecture. EFI on the larger motors, carburetted on the smaller portables, with corrosion-protection across the range that matters when the boat lives at a dock on Lake Superior or anywhere with hard freshwater. The motor handles a cold-water October morning predictably; the fuel curve doesn't surprise you.

From Reyco, you also get the dealer side of the package. Lee in the service department handles the marine bench. Lynn manages the parts counter. Ron, our parts specialist, knows the Mercury accessory catalogue cold. Casey, Aaron, and Kory on the sales floor can walk you through fit on your specific hull.

Drop into 11 White Oak Drive East to see one on the floor.

Reyco's been on White Oak Drive for more than 60 years. The dealership has grown across boats, motors, and lawn-and-snow equipment, but the core of what we do hasn't changed — we sell what we'd put on our own gear, we service what we sell on our own bench, and we back the warranty paperwork in-house so the customer doesn't have to chase it. That's the part of an authorised-dealer purchase that doesn't show up on the price tag, and it's the reason most customers in Sault Ste. Marie don't think twice about where they go for marine and outdoor power equipment. We've earned that trust one boat, one motor, and one season at a time. The relationship is meant to run for years — not just the duration of a sale. When the unit needs attention, you stop in or you call, and someone on the team who knows you answers. That's the dealer side that customers tell us they value most.

Name: 2024 Mercury 15 HP FourStroke Tiller Electric Start Outboard Motor
Short description: Mercury's 15 hp FourStroke sized as small kicker / tiller for a 14-foot Lund or aluminum jon. Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer — we register the warranty in-house and service it on our bench at White Oak Drive.
Description:
For boats running the Sault, the 2024 Mercury 15 hp in small kicker / tiller configuration is one of the motors Reyco recommends most. The reason is simple: this is freshwater Mercury knows well — the corrosion package, the cold-start fuel management, and the run-quality at lower water temperatures are all dialled for the conditions our customers actually fish.

On a 14-foot Lund or aluminum jon, this motor lands in the operating sweet spot. Underpowered hulls plow; overpowered hulls stress the transom. The 15 hp puts the rig where its designer intended — that's the reason small kicker / tiller is the most-recommended class for this boat size.

As an authorised Mercury dealer, Reyco handles warranty registration in-house, services on our own bench, and stocks the consumables on the parts counter. Lee on the service bench knows the current Mercury internals; Lynn at the parts counter and Ron behind it pull the spark plugs, lower-unit oil, and props off the shelf.

Stop by the showroom and Lee on the service bench can walk you through rigging.

What separates a dealer from a marketplace listing is what happens after the sale. At Reyco, that means warranty registration handled the day you take delivery, break-in or first-service slots booked in our own shop, and a parts counter that already has the consumables for this unit on the shelf. We've been doing the work in Sault Ste. Marie for more than 60 years — long enough to have seen multiple generations of Mercury, Princecraft, Minn Kota, Cannon, and Humminbird product redesigns roll through the same showroom. The relationships we've built with the brand reps, the technical support lines, and the dealer networks are part of what we bring to every sale. That's the value of buying from a long-tenured authorised dealer. When something needs warranty attention, we know who to call and we get an answer the same day in most cases. That's not something a box-store competitor can match.

Name: 2022 Mercury 5 HP FourStroke Outboard Motor
Short description: Mercury's 5 hp FourStroke sized as portable for a 12-foot tinny, a dinghy, or a sailboat auxiliary. Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer — we register the warranty in-house and service it on our bench at White Oak Drive.
Description:
Customers shopping a Mercury 5 hp ask the same question — what's the 2022 build going to do over five-plus seasons? For portable power on a 12-foot tinny, a dinghy, or a sailboat auxiliary, the FourStroke engineering is the answer: refined fuel management, dependable cold-start, and the corrosion package Mercury ships across the modern range.

Casey's read on this output: it's the motor that does the most for the most boats in our customer base. The back-lake brook-trout chaser is the buyer it serves best. The motor is sized for the hull, the fuel burn is reasonable, and the cold-start is what Mercury customers in the back lakes off the Trans-Canada expect.

After the sale: we handle the warranty paperwork, service it on our own bench, and keep the consumables in stock. Lee in the service department, Lynn managing the parts counter, Ron pulling the accessory orders. That's the dealer side of the deal — the part you don't get when you buy a motor from a box-store or off a marketplace listing.

Speak to Ron at the parts counter for accessories that pair with this unit.

There's a reason customers in Northern Ontario keep coming back to Reyco for marine purchases — the in-house service relationship is the part that matters when the boat is out on water, the lake is cold, and the customer needs help. Lee in the service department, Lynn managing the parts counter, Ron pulling accessories, Casey, Aaron, and Kory on the sales floor — that's the team that backs the unit you buy from us. We see most boats back in for shoulder-season service every year, and that's how the relationship is meant to run. The customer who buys here doesn't end up calling a 1-800 line when something needs attention; they call us, and the call gets answered by someone who's already been on the boat. That's the kind of dealer-customer continuity that takes 60-plus years on a single street to build, and it's not something we take for granted.

Name: 2023 Mercury 115 HP Pro XS Outboard Motor
Short description: Mercury's 115 hp Pro XS sized as mid-large main for an 18- to 20-foot aluminum or fibreglass. Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer — we register the warranty in-house and service it on our bench at White Oak Drive.
Description:
Of all the Mercury Pro XS outboards in the mid-large main class on our floor right now, the 2023 115 hp is the unit we keep stocked longest because it walks out the door fastest. Current engineering, factory-authorised through Reyco.

Sized for an 18- to 20-foot aluminum or fibreglass, recommended for the serious tournament angler, and built to handle a Lake Superior shoulder-season morning the way Mercury's Pro XS line has been refined to. It's a motor we sell to first-time outboard buyers and to customers on their fifth Mercury — and the conversation is the same either way.

From Reyco, the package includes warranty registration, in-house service from Lee in the service department, and the parts-counter coverage Lynn and Ron run on Mercury consumables. We register the motor before it leaves the lot, we book the break-in service before the customer asks, and we see the boat back in the shoulder season for end-of-year winterization.

Book a service slot with Lee in the service department before next launch.

Reyco runs as an authorised dealer for the brands we carry because the warranty backing and the in-house service are what make the long-term ownership work. We register the warranty before the unit leaves the lot. We book the break-in service before the customer asks. We carry the consumables on the parts counter so when something needs replacing, the customer walks out with the part the same day. That's the dealer-side value, and it's the reason customers in Northern Ontario keep buying from us across decades — not just one purchase. Casey, Aaron, and Kory on the sales floor can talk you through any of this; Lynn and Ron handle the parts side; Lee handles service. It's a small team that knows the customer, and we like it that way. We've watched generations of families buy boats here — fathers, sons, and now grandkids — and that's the kind of relationship a 60-plus-year dealership earns.

Name: 2023 Mercury 20 HP FourStroke Outboard Motor
Short description: Mercury's 20 hp FourStroke sized as mid-range tiller for a 14- to 16-foot aluminum. Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer — we register the warranty in-house and service it on our bench at White Oak Drive.
Description:
The 2023 Mercury 20 hp FourStroke is the kind of motor you can pull off the rack, set on the transom of a 14- to 16-foot aluminum, and be fishing inside of five minutes. That's the use-case Reyco sees this output turn up for most often — primary motor for the cottage owner who needs more push, headed somewhere out on Lake Superior.

The FourStroke 20 is sized as mid-range tiller-class power. What that means in plain English: it'll push the hulls it's meant for at the speeds those hulls are designed to run, without the engine working overtime. Mercury's FourStroke line has been refined long enough that the cold-start behaviour is what we'd expect — the boat starts on the first or second pull, even on a chilly first-launch of the season.

Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer. When you buy this motor from us, we register the warranty in-house and Lee in the service department is the same tech who'll see it again at break-in service or end-of-season winterization. We carry the spark plugs, lower-unit oil, propellers and pull-cord assemblies for the FourStroke and Pro XS lines on the parts counter — Lynn manages the parts counter and Ron knows the Mercury accessory line cold.

Casey carries Mercury because the warranty backs the customer in a way that matters when the dealer is the one a buyer trusts to make it right. Speak to Ron at the parts counter for accessories that pair with this unit.

Reyco's been on White Oak Drive for more than 60 years. The dealership has grown across boats, motors, and lawn-and-snow equipment, but the core of what we do hasn't changed — we sell what we'd put on our own gear, we service what we sell on our own bench, and we back the warranty paperwork in-house so the customer doesn't have to chase it. That's the part of an authorised-dealer purchase that doesn't show up on the price tag, and it's the reason most customers in Sault Ste. Marie don't think twice about where they go for marine and outdoor power equipment. We've earned that trust one boat, one motor, and one season at a time. The relationship is meant to run for years — not just the duration of a sale. When the unit needs attention, you stop in or you call, and someone on the team who knows you answers. That's the dealer side that customers tell us they value most.

Name: 2021 Mercury 2.5 HP FourStroke Outboard Motor
Short description: Mercury's 2.5 hp FourStroke sized as portable for a 12-foot tinny, a dinghy, or a sailboat auxiliary. Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer — we register the warranty in-house and service it on our bench at White Oak Drive.
Description:
For the cottage runabout owner, the 2021 Mercury 2.5 hp FourStroke is the unit we'd hand you the keys to first. The portable output is designed for a 12-foot tinny, a dinghy, or a sailboat auxiliary — exactly the hull most 2021 buyers are putting it on.

There's nothing exotic about this motor. It's Mercury's well-proven FourStroke engineering: reliable cold-water start, predictable fuel burn, and the kind of run-quality that means the boat goes the distance you need it to without drama. We've sold enough 2.5 hp Mercurys around the St. Marys River to know the failure modes — and there aren't many.

What you get when you buy from Reyco: factory-authorised registration, service from Lee on the service bench (engines, boats, ATV — he covers it), and a parts counter with the consumables for this motor already stocked. Lynn manages the parts shelf, Ron handles the Mercury accessory pulls. If the boat needs a winterization at end of season, we book it in and turn it around in the shoulder-season window before the spring rush.

Book a service slot with Lee in the service department before next launch.

What separates a dealer from a marketplace listing is what happens after the sale. At Reyco, that means warranty registration handled the day you take delivery, break-in or first-service slots booked in our own shop, and a parts counter that already has the consumables for this unit on the shelf. We've been doing the work in Sault Ste. Marie for more than 60 years — long enough to have seen multiple generations of Mercury, Princecraft, Minn Kota, Cannon, and Humminbird product redesigns roll through the same showroom. The relationships we've built with the brand reps, the technical support lines, and the dealer networks are part of what we bring to every sale. That's the value of buying from a long-tenured authorised dealer. When something needs warranty attention, we know who to call and we get an answer the same day in most cases. That's not something a box-store competitor can match.

Name: 2022 Mercury 8 HP FourStroke Outboard Motor
Short description: Mercury's 8 hp FourStroke sized as small kicker / tiller for a 14-foot Lund or aluminum jon. Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer — we register the warranty in-house and service it on our bench at White Oak Drive.
Description:
On Whitefish Bay, the Mercury 8 hp FourStroke is one of the motors Reyco carries because the difference between a 2022 build that starts cold and one that doesn't is the difference between fishing and hauling the boat home. That's the calculus when you run a marine business in a town where the customer doesn't have a backup if the kicker dies.

This is small kicker / tiller-class power, sized for a 14-foot Lund or aluminum jon. The FourStroke designation means it's part of Mercury's modern four-stroke product line — quieter than a two-stroke, runs unleaded out of any can in the shed, and doesn't smoke up the cottage. For the cottage daily-driver, that matters more than spec-sheet horsepower numbers.

Reyco registers the warranty in-house and we service every Mercury we sell on our own bench. Lee in the service department handles the marine work, and the parts counter — Lynn and Ron — keeps the FourStroke consumables on the shelf so when you need a plug or a prop pin, you walk out with it.

Drop into 11 White Oak Drive East to see one on the floor. Casey, Aaron, or Kory on the sales floor can walk you through specifics.

There's a reason customers in Northern Ontario keep coming back to Reyco for marine purchases — the in-house service relationship is the part that matters when the boat is out on water, the lake is cold, and the customer needs help. Lee in the service department, Lynn managing the parts counter, Ron pulling accessories, Casey, Aaron, and Kory on the sales floor — that's the team that backs the unit you buy from us. We see most boats back in for shoulder-season service every year, and that's how the relationship is meant to run. The customer who buys here doesn't end up calling a 1-800 line when something needs attention; they call us, and the call gets answered by someone who's already been on the boat. That's the kind of dealer-customer continuity that takes 60-plus years on a single street to build, and it's not something we take for granted.

Name: 2023 Mercury 3.5 HP FourStroke Outboard Motor
Short description: Mercury's 3.5 hp FourStroke sized as portable for a 12-foot tinny, a dinghy, or a sailboat auxiliary. Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer — we register the warranty in-house and service it on our bench at White Oak Drive.
Description:
The 3.5 hp Mercury sits in the portable sweet spot — the 2023 build is the size of motor that does the most for the most customers in our base. We move more 3.5-class Mercurys through this dealership than most other single outputs in the lineup, and there's a reason: it fits a 12-foot tinny, a dinghy, or a sailboat auxiliary cleanly without overpowering or underpowering the rig.

What's behind the spec sheet: this is the 2023 model-year unit, in current Mercury build. The FourStroke / Pro XS engineering — depending on the trim — covers the cold-start reliability, the fuel-management refinement, and the run-quality that customers expect from Mercury. We don't fit these to the wrong hull; if you tell us what you're running, Casey will tell you whether this is the right output or whether you should look at the next-step-up unit on the floor.

On the service side: Lee in the service department handles the marine bench, the parts counter keeps the consumables in stock, and we register the warranty before the motor leaves the lot. That's the difference between buying from an authorised dealer and buying online.

Stop by the showroom and Lee on the service bench can walk you through rigging.

Reyco runs as an authorised dealer for the brands we carry because the warranty backing and the in-house service are what make the long-term ownership work. We register the warranty before the unit leaves the lot. We book the break-in service before the customer asks. We carry the consumables on the parts counter so when something needs replacing, the customer walks out with the part the same day. That's the dealer-side value, and it's the reason customers in Northern Ontario keep buying from us across decades — not just one purchase. Casey, Aaron, and Kory on the sales floor can talk you through any of this; Lynn and Ron handle the parts side; Lee handles service. It's a small team that knows the customer, and we like it that way. We've watched generations of families buy boats here — fathers, sons, and now grandkids — and that's the kind of relationship a 60-plus-year dealership earns.

Description:
When a customer walks in asking what 2025 Mercury they should put on a 16- to 18-foot aluminum runabout or fishing boat, the 60 hp in mid-power main configuration is the answer Casey gives most often in this output range. The FourStroke build fits the hull and the use-case cleanly.

It comes down to fit. Underpowered, the boat plows; overpowered, the rig stresses the transom and burns fuel for no return. The 60 hp lands in the operating zone the hull was designed for — that's why we recommend it for the family fishing-boat owner on a typical Reyco-spec rig.

On the back end: we're an authorised Mercury dealer, which means warranty registration and factory-authorised service in-house. Lee runs the service bench, Lynn manages the parts counter, Ron handles the Mercury accessory line. We carry the consumables for the FourStroke engines on the shelf so if you stop in for a plug or a prop pin, you walk out with it the same day.

Book a service slot with Lee in the service department before next launch.

What separates a dealer from a marketplace listing is what happens after the sale. At Reyco, that means warranty registration handled the day you take delivery, break-in or first-service slots booked in our own shop, and a parts counter that already has the consumables for this unit on the shelf. We've been doing the work in Sault Ste. Marie for more than 60 years — long enough to have seen multiple generations of Mercury, Princecraft, Minn Kota, Cannon, and Humminbird product redesigns roll through the same showroom. The relationships we've built with the brand reps, the technical support lines, and the dealer networks are part of what we bring to every sale. That's the value of buying from a long-tenured authorised dealer. When something needs warranty attention, we know who to call and we get an answer the same day in most cases. That's not something a box-store competitor can match.

25 hp. 2024 model year. FourStroke. That's the bare spec sheet — and on the showroom floor, this motor is the mid-range tiller option Reyco recommends first to the cottage owner who needs more push on a 14- to 16-foot aluminum.

Past the spec sheet, what matters is how the motor behaves on the water. Lynn manages the counter; Ron and the rest of the parts team know which spark plug, which lower-unit oil, which prop fits which rigging.

Drop into 11 White Oak Drive East to see one on the floor.

There's a reason customers in Northern Ontario keep coming back to Reyco for marine purchases — the in-house service relationship is the part that matters when the boat is out on water, the lake is cold, and the customer needs help. Lee in the service department, Lynn managing the parts counter, Ron pulling accessories, Casey, Aaron, and Kory on the sales floor — that's the team that backs the unit you buy from us. We see most boats back in for shoulder-season service every year, and that's how the relationship is meant to run. The customer who buys here doesn't end up calling a 1-800 line when something needs attention; they call us, and the call gets answered by someone who's already been on the boat. That's the kind of dealer-customer continuity that takes 60-plus years on a single street to build, and it's not something we take for granted.

This is portable-class power, sized for a 12-foot tinny, a dinghy, or a sailboat auxiliary. The use case is the cottage runabout owner on water like the Sault — the kind of running where the motor earns its keep over a multi-year horizon.

Reyco's been a Mercury dealer long enough that we've seen the 4 hp class of outboard go through three Mercury product redesigns. The current 2022 build is in our experience the most refined of those generations — quieter, smoother fuel curve, better cold-start. Past the marketing, it's a motor we trust to leave the lot and start every spring.

Stop by the showroom and Lee on the service bench can walk you through rigging.

Reyco runs as an authorised dealer for the brands we carry because the warranty backing and the in-house service are what make the long-term ownership work. We register the warranty before the unit leaves the lot. We book the break-in service before the customer asks. We carry the consumables on the parts counter so when something needs replacing, the customer walks out with the part the same day. That's the dealer-side value, and it's the reason customers in Northern Ontario keep buying from us across decades — not just one purchase. Casey, Aaron, and Kory on the sales floor can talk you through any of this; Lynn and Ron handle the parts side; Lee handles service. It's a small team that knows the customer, and we like it that way. We've watched generations of families buy boats here — fathers, sons, and now grandkids — and that's the kind of relationship a 60-plus-year dealership earns.

Name: 2023 Mercury 25 HP FourStroke Outboard Motor
Short description: Mercury's 25 hp FourStroke sized as mid-range tiller for a 14- to 16-foot aluminum. Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer — we register the warranty in-house and service it on our bench at White Oak Drive.
Description:
This 2023 Mercury 25 hp is current-generation FourStroke engineering, factory-authorised through Reyco. Mid-range tiller power for a 14- to 16-foot aluminum, and that's the configuration we sell more than any other in this output range.

The FourStroke line — when applicable — is Mercury's modern four-stroke architecture. EFI on the larger motors, carburetted on the smaller portables, with corrosion-protection across the range that matters when the boat lives at a dock on Lake Superior or anywhere with hard freshwater. The motor handles an early-spring trolling run predictably; the fuel curve doesn't surprise you.

From Reyco, you also get the dealer side of the package. Lee in the service department handles the marine bench. Lynn manages the parts counter. Ron, our parts specialist, knows the Mercury accessory catalogue cold. Casey, Aaron, and Kory on the sales floor can walk you through fit on your specific hull.

Speak to Ron at the parts counter for accessories that pair with this unit.

Reyco's been on White Oak Drive for more than 60 years. The dealership has grown across boats, motors, and lawn-and-snow equipment, but the core of what we do hasn't changed — we sell what we'd put on our own gear, we service what we sell on our own bench, and we back the warranty paperwork in-house so the customer doesn't have to chase it. That's the part of an authorised-dealer purchase that doesn't show up on the price tag, and it's the reason most customers in Sault Ste. Marie don't think twice about where they go for marine and outdoor power equipment. We've earned that trust one boat, one motor, and one season at a time. The relationship is meant to run for years — not just the duration of a sale. When the unit needs attention, you stop in or you call, and someone on the team who knows you answers. That's the dealer side that customers tell us they value most.

Name: 2022 Mercury 20 HP FourStroke Outboard Motor
Short description: Mercury's 20 hp FourStroke sized as mid-range tiller for a 14- to 16-foot aluminum. Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer — we register the warranty in-house and service it on our bench at White Oak Drive.
Description:
For boats running the Goulais River area, the 2022 Mercury 20 hp in mid-range tiller configuration is one of the motors Reyco recommends most. The reason is simple: this is freshwater Mercury knows well — the corrosion package, the cold-start fuel management, and the run-quality at lower water temperatures are all dialled for the conditions our customers actually fish.

On a 14- to 16-foot aluminum, this motor lands in the operating sweet spot. Underpowered hulls plow; overpowered hulls stress the transom. The 20 hp puts the rig where its designer intended — that's the reason mid-range tiller is the most-recommended class for this boat size.

As an authorised Mercury dealer, Reyco handles warranty registration in-house, services on our own bench, and stocks the consumables on the parts counter. Lee on the service bench knows the current Mercury internals; Lynn at the parts counter and Ron behind it pull the spark plugs, lower-unit oil, and props off the shelf.

Book a service slot with Lee in the service department before next launch.

What separates a dealer from a marketplace listing is what happens after the sale. At Reyco, that means warranty registration handled the day you take delivery, break-in or first-service slots booked in our own shop, and a parts counter that already has the consumables for this unit on the shelf. We've been doing the work in Sault Ste. Marie for more than 60 years — long enough to have seen multiple generations of Mercury, Princecraft, Minn Kota, Cannon, and Humminbird product redesigns roll through the same showroom. The relationships we've built with the brand reps, the technical support lines, and the dealer networks are part of what we bring to every sale. That's the value of buying from a long-tenured authorised dealer. When something needs warranty attention, we know who to call and we get an answer the same day in most cases. That's not something a box-store competitor can match.

Name: 2022 Mercury 30 HP FourStroke Outboard Motor
Short description: Mercury's 30 hp FourStroke sized as mid-range tiller for a 14- to 16-foot aluminum. Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer — we register the warranty in-house and service it on our bench at White Oak Drive.
Description:
Of all the Mercury FourStroke outboards in the mid-range tiller class on our floor right now, the 2022 30 hp is the unit we keep stocked longest because it walks out the door fastest. Current engineering, factory-authorised through Reyco.

Sized for a 14- to 16-foot aluminum, recommended for the cottage owner who needs more push, and built to handle an ice-out April launch the way Mercury's FourStroke line has been refined to. It's a motor we sell to first-time outboard buyers and to customers on their fifth Mercury — and the conversation is the same either way.

From Reyco, the package includes warranty registration, in-house service from Lee in the service department, and the parts-counter coverage Lynn and Ron run on Mercury consumables. We register the motor before it leaves the lot, we book the break-in service before the customer asks, and we see the boat back in the shoulder season for end-of-year winterization.

Stop by the showroom and Lee on the service bench can walk you through rigging.

Reyco runs as an authorised dealer for the brands we carry because the warranty backing and the in-house service are what make the long-term ownership work. We register the warranty before the unit leaves the lot. We book the break-in service before the customer asks. We carry the consumables on the parts counter so when something needs replacing, the customer walks out with the part the same day. That's the dealer-side value, and it's the reason customers in Northern Ontario keep buying from us across decades — not just one purchase. Casey, Aaron, and Kory on the sales floor can talk you through any of this; Lynn and Ron handle the parts side; Lee handles service. It's a small team that knows the customer, and we like it that way. We've watched generations of families buy boats here — fathers, sons, and now grandkids — and that's the kind of relationship a 60-plus-year dealership earns.

I'd rather lose a sale than sell the wrong unit — come in, talk it through, and let's make sure it's the right call for what you're doing.

Name: 2024 Mercury 4 HP FourStroke Outboard Motor
Short description: Mercury's 4 hp FourStroke sized as portable for a 12-foot tinny, a dinghy, or a sailboat auxiliary. Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer — we register the warranty in-house and service it on our bench at White Oak Drive.
Description:
The 2024 Mercury 4 hp FourStroke is the kind of motor you can pull off the rack, set on the transom of a 12-foot tinny, a dinghy, or a sailboat auxiliary, and be fishing inside of five minutes. That's the use-case Reyco sees this output turn up for most often — primary motor for the cottage runabout owner, headed somewhere out on Whitefish Bay.

The FourStroke 4 is sized as portable-class power. What that means in plain English: it'll push the hulls it's meant for at the speeds those hulls are designed to run, without the engine working overtime. Mercury's FourStroke line has been refined long enough that the cold-start behaviour is what we'd expect — the boat starts on the first or second pull, even on a frosty pre-dawn start.

Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer. When you buy this motor from us, we register the warranty in-house and Lee in the service department is the same tech who'll see it again at break-in service or end-of-season winterization. We carry the spark plugs, lower-unit oil, propellers and pull-cord assemblies for the FourStroke and Pro XS lines on the parts counter — Lynn manages the parts counter and Ron knows the Mercury accessory line cold.

Casey carries Mercury because the warranty backs the customer in a way that matters when the dealer is the one a buyer trusts to make it right. Drop into 11 White Oak Drive East to see one on the floor.

Reyco's been on White Oak Drive for more than 60 years. The dealership has grown across boats, motors, and lawn-and-snow equipment, but the core of what we do hasn't changed — we sell what we'd put on our own gear, we service what we sell on our own bench, and we back the warranty paperwork in-house so the customer doesn't have to chase it. That's the part of an authorised-dealer purchase that doesn't show up on the price tag, and it's the reason most customers in Sault Ste. Marie don't think twice about where they go for marine and outdoor power equipment. We've earned that trust one boat, one motor, and one season at a time. The relationship is meant to run for years — not just the duration of a sale. When the unit needs attention, you stop in or you call, and someone on the team who knows you answers. That's the dealer side that customers tell us they value most.

Name: 2024 Mercury 6 HP FourStroke Outboard Motor
Short description: Mercury's 6 hp FourStroke sized as portable for a 12-foot tinny, a dinghy, or a sailboat auxiliary. Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer — we register the warranty in-house and service it on our bench at White Oak Drive.
Description:
For the cottage runabout owner, the 2024 Mercury 6 hp FourStroke is the unit we'd hand you the keys to first. The portable output is designed for a 12-foot tinny, a dinghy, or a sailboat auxiliary — exactly the hull most 2024 buyers are putting it on.

There's nothing exotic about this motor. It's Mercury's well-proven FourStroke engineering: reliable cold-water start, predictable fuel burn, and the kind of run-quality that means the boat goes the distance you need it to without drama. We've sold enough 6 hp Mercurys around Algoma to know the failure modes — and there aren't many.

What you get when you buy from Reyco: factory-authorised registration, service from Lee on the service bench (engines, boats, ATV — he covers it), and a parts counter with the consumables for this motor already stocked. Lynn manages the parts shelf, Ron handles the Mercury accessory pulls. If the boat needs a winterization at end of season, we book it in and turn it around in the shoulder-season window before the spring rush.

Stop by the showroom and Lee on the service bench can walk you through rigging.

What separates a dealer from a marketplace listing is what happens after the sale. At Reyco, that means warranty registration handled the day you take delivery, break-in or first-service slots booked in our own shop, and a parts counter that already has the consumables for this unit on the shelf. We've been doing the work in Sault Ste. Marie for more than 60 years — long enough to have seen multiple generations of Mercury, Princecraft, Minn Kota, Cannon, and Humminbird product redesigns roll through the same showroom. The relationships we've built with the brand reps, the technical support lines, and the dealer networks are part of what we bring to every sale. That's the value of buying from a long-tenured authorised dealer. When something needs warranty attention, we know who to call and we get an answer the same day in most cases. That's not something a box-store competitor can match.

Name: 2024 Princecraft Resorter 160 BT Aluminum Fishing Boat
Short description: 2024 Princecraft Resorter aluminum fishing boat. Authorised Princecraft dealer at Reyco — sales, rigging, and service in-house. See it on the lot at White Oak Drive.
Description:
The 2024 Princecraft Resorter 160 Bt is an aluminum fishing boat — a tiller-or-console-friendly hull sized for the back-lake and shoulder-of-the-Great-Lakes water that the Trans-Canada cottage country customers fish. Princecraft's aluminum lineup has been a Canadian fishing-boat staple for decades; we stock the Resorter class because it's the size that fits the most customer use cases.

The conversation Reyco has with the customer shopping this hull is about fit — what motor, what trolling-motor mount, what electronics package. We're authorised on Mercury, Minn Kota, and Humminbird, so the rigging stays in-house: Casey will spec the package, Lee in the service department fits and water-tests it, Lynn manages the parts counter for the consumables, Ron handles accessory orders.

For the multi-species weekend fisherman, this is the size of aluminum hull Reyco recommends most often. The Princecraft build quality, the warranty that comes with an authorised-dealer purchase, and the in-house rigging are the three reasons we keep this hull on the floor.

Stop by the showroom and Lee on the service bench can walk you through rigging. Casey can walk you through Mercury powering options on the sales floor.

There's a reason customers in Northern Ontario keep coming back to Reyco for marine purchases — the in-house service relationship is the part that matters when the boat is out on water, the lake is cold, and the customer needs help. Lee in the service department, Lynn managing the parts counter, Ron pulling accessories, Casey, Aaron, and Kory on the sales floor — that's the team that backs the unit you buy from us. We see most boats back in for shoulder-season service every year, and that's how the relationship is meant to run. The customer who buys here doesn't end up calling a 1-800 line when something needs attention; they call us, and the call gets answered by someone who's already been on the boat. That's the kind of dealer-customer continuity that takes 60-plus years on a single street to build, and it's not something we take for granted.

Name: 2023 Mercury 6 HP FourStroke Outboard Motor
Short description: Mercury's 6 hp FourStroke sized as portable for a 12-foot tinny, a dinghy, or a sailboat auxiliary. Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer — we register the warranty in-house and service it on our bench at White Oak Drive.
Description:
The 6 hp Mercury sits in the portable sweet spot — the 2023 build is the size of motor that does the most for the most customers in our base. We move more 6-class Mercurys through this dealership than most other single outputs in the lineup, and there's a reason: it fits a 12-foot tinny, a dinghy, or a sailboat auxiliary cleanly without overpowering or underpowering the rig.

What's behind the spec sheet: this is the 2023 model-year unit, in current Mercury build. The FourStroke / Pro XS engineering — depending on the trim — covers the cold-start reliability, the fuel-management refinement, and the run-quality that customers expect from Mercury. We don't fit these to the wrong hull; if you tell us what you're running, Casey will tell you whether this is the right output or whether you should look at the next-step-up unit on the floor.

On the service side: Lee in the service department handles the marine bench, the parts counter keeps the consumables in stock, and we register the warranty before the motor leaves the lot. That's the difference between buying from an authorised dealer and buying online.

Book a service slot with Lee in the service department before next launch.

Reyco runs as an authorised dealer for the brands we carry because the warranty backing and the in-house service are what make the long-term ownership work. We register the warranty before the unit leaves the lot. We book the break-in service before the customer asks. We carry the consumables on the parts counter so when something needs replacing, the customer walks out with the part the same day. That's the dealer-side value, and it's the reason customers in Northern Ontario keep buying from us across decades — not just one purchase. Casey, Aaron, and Kory on the sales floor can talk you through any of this; Lynn and Ron handle the parts side; Lee handles service. It's a small team that knows the customer, and we like it that way. We've watched generations of families buy boats here — fathers, sons, and now grandkids — and that's the kind of relationship a 60-plus-year dealership earns.

Name: 2021 Mercury 5 HP FourStroke Outboard Motor

Description:
On Northern Ontario, the 5 hp class of Mercury outboard is the one that does the most work for the most boats. This 2021 FourStroke build is sized as portable power for a 12-foot tinny, a dinghy, or a sailboat auxiliary — the configuration we see in our customer fleet more than any other.

What you're getting: the 2021 FourStroke build, with Mercury's current cold-start, electronic-fuel-management, and corrosion-protection package as standard. The motor doesn't care whether you're the cottage runabout owner or the get-home-backup buyer — it'll do the same job either way.

Reyco's been an authorised Mercury dealer for the better part of three generations of motor design. That means: we register the warranty in-house, we service every motor we sell on our own bench, and Lee in the service department knows the FourStroke internals as well as anyone in Northern Ontario. Parts? Lynn manages the counter; Ron handles the accessory pulls. Most consumables for this motor are already on our shelves.

Drop into 11 White Oak Drive East to see one on the floor.

Reyco's been on White Oak Drive for more than 60 years. The dealership has grown across boats, motors, and lawn-and-snow equipment, but the core of what we do hasn't changed — we sell what we'd put on our own gear, we service what we sell on our own bench, and we back the warranty paperwork in-house so the customer doesn't have to chase it. That's the part of an authorised-dealer purchase that doesn't show up on the price tag, and it's the reason most customers in Sault Ste. Marie don't think twice about where they go for marine and outdoor power equipment. We've earned that trust one boat, one motor, and one season at a time. The relationship is meant to run for years — not just the duration of a sale. When the unit needs attention, you stop in or you call, and someone on the team who knows you answers. That's the dealer side that customers tell us they value most.

Name: 2026 Mercury 115 HP Pro XS Outboard Motor
Short description: Mercury's 115 hp Pro XS sized as mid-large main for an 18- to 20-foot aluminum or fibreglass. Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer — we register the warranty in-house and service it on our bench at White Oak Drive.
Description:
When a customer walks in asking what 2026 Mercury they should put on an 18- to 20-foot aluminum or fibreglass, the 115 hp in mid-large main configuration is the answer Casey gives most often in this output range. The Pro XS build fits the hull and the use-case cleanly.

It comes down to fit. Underpowered, the boat plows; overpowered, the rig stresses the transom and burns fuel for no return. The 115 hp lands in the operating zone the hull was designed for — that's why we recommend it for the serious tournament angler on a typical Reyco-spec rig.

On the back end: we're an authorised Mercury dealer, which means warranty registration and factory-authorised service in-house. Lee runs the service bench, Lynn manages the parts counter, Ron handles the Mercury accessory line. We carry the consumables for the Pro XS engines on the shelf so if you stop in for a plug or a prop pin, you walk out with it the same day.

Stop by the showroom and Lee on the service bench can walk you through rigging.

What separates a dealer from a marketplace listing is what happens after the sale. At Reyco, that means warranty registration handled the day you take delivery, break-in or first-service slots booked in our own shop, and a parts counter that already has the consumables for this unit on the shelf. We've been doing the work in Sault Ste. Marie for more than 60 years — long enough to have seen multiple generations of Mercury, Princecraft, Minn Kota, Cannon, and Humminbird product redesigns roll through the same showroom. The relationships we've built with the brand reps, the technical support lines, and the dealer networks are part of what we bring to every sale. That's the value of buying from a long-tenured authorised dealer. When something needs warranty attention, we know who to call and we get an answer the same day in most cases. That's not something a box-store competitor can match.

Name: 2026 Princecraft Vectra 21-ft Pontoon
Short description: 2026 Princecraft Vectra 21-ft pontoon — Reyco is an authorised Princecraft dealer in Sault Ste. Marie. Available off the lot at White Oak Drive. Talk to Casey about fit on your lake.
Description:
The 2026 Princecraft Vectra 21-ft pontoon is the kind of boat the cottage country around the back lakes off the Trans-Canada runs on summer weekends — built for relaxed cruising, family swims, sundown trolling, and the back-deck dinner that follows. Princecraft is a Canadian-built brand, and the Vectra sits in the part of the lineup we move most often through Reyco.

Casey carries Princecraft because the Canadian build matters in the back lakes off the Trans-Canada. The aluminum construction, the fit-and-finish on the deck and rails, and the Princecraft after-sales network are all reasons we've stocked the brand longer than most lineups on the floor. We're an authorised Princecraft dealer — the boat is registered, rigged, and water-tested through our service department before it leaves the lot.

Lee in the service department handles the rigging and outboard fit (we'll spec a Mercury that matches the hull, since we're authorised on both brands). Lynn manages the parts counter for the consumables that come up over the season — fuel filters, lower-unit oil, prop hardware. Ron handles the parts orders for accessories like fish-finder mounting, anchor systems, and covers.

The Vectra is a pontoon Reyco specs out as sundown-and-tubing configuration. Casey, Aaron, or Kory on the sales floor can walk you through trim options, powering decisions, and delivery timeline. Book a service slot with Lee in the service department before next launch.

There's a reason customers in Northern Ontario keep coming back to Reyco for marine purchases — the in-house service relationship is the part that matters when the boat is out on water, the lake is cold, and the customer needs help. Lee in the service department, Lynn managing the parts counter, Ron pulling accessories, Casey, Aaron, and Kory on the sales floor — that's the team that backs the unit you buy from us. We see most boats back in for shoulder-season service every year, and that's how the relationship is meant to run. The customer who buys here doesn't end up calling a 1-800 line when something needs attention; they call us, and the call gets answered by someone who's already been on the boat. That's the kind of dealer-customer continuity that takes 60-plus years on a single street to build, and it's not something we take for granted.

Name: 2025 Mercury 75 HP FourStroke Outboard Motor
Short description: Mercury's 75 hp FourStroke sized as mid-large main for an 18- to 20-foot aluminum or fibreglass. Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer — we register the warranty in-house and service it on our bench at White Oak Drive.
Description:
Most customers who buy a 2025 Mercury 75 hp from Reyco see Lee in the service department within the first year — not for repair, but for the break-in service the FourStroke line benefits from. That's the shape of the relationship around an authorised-dealer purchase, and it's a good sign for the buyer.

This is mid-large main-class power, sized for an 18- to 20-foot aluminum or fibreglass. The use case is the serious tournament angler on water like the Goulais River area — the kind of running where the motor earns its keep over a multi-year horizon.

Reyco's been a Mercury dealer long enough that we've seen the 75 hp class of outboard go through three Mercury product redesigns. The current 2025 build is in our experience the most refined of those generations — quieter, smoother fuel curve, better cold-start. Past the marketing, it's a motor we trust to leave the lot and start every spring.

Book a service slot with Lee in the service department before next launch.

Reyco runs as an authorised dealer for the brands we carry because the warranty backing and the in-house service are what make the long-term ownership work. We register the warranty before the unit leaves the lot. We book the break-in service before the customer asks. We carry the consumables on the parts counter so when something needs replacing, the customer walks out with the part the same day. That's the dealer-side value, and it's the reason customers in Northern Ontario keep buying from us across decades — not just one purchase. Casey, Aaron, and Kory on the sales floor can talk you through any of this; Lynn and Ron handle the parts side; Lee handles service. It's a small team that knows the customer, and we like it that way. We've watched generations of families buy boats here — fathers, sons, and now grandkids — and that's the kind of relationship a 60-plus-year dealership earns.

Name: 2025 Princecraft Amarok 166 WS Aluminum Fishing Boat
Short description: 2025 Princecraft Amarok aluminum fishing boat. Authorised Princecraft dealer at Reyco — sales, rigging, and service in-house. See it on the lot at White Oak Drive.
Description:
The 2025 Princecraft Amarok 166 Ws is an aluminum fishing boat — a tiller-or-console-friendly hull sized for the back-lake and shoulder-of-the-Great-Lakes water that Lake Superior customers fish. Princecraft's aluminum lineup has been a Canadian fishing-boat staple for decades; we stock the Amarok class because it's the size that fits the most customer use cases.

The conversation Reyco has with the customer shopping this hull is about fit — what motor, what trolling-motor mount, what electronics package. We're authorised on Mercury, Minn Kota, and Humminbird, so the rigging stays in-house: Casey will spec the package, Lee in the service department fits and water-tests it, Lynn manages the parts counter for the consumables, Ron handles accessory orders.

For the lake trout chaser, this is the size of aluminum hull Reyco recommends most often. The Princecraft build quality, the warranty that comes with an authorised-dealer purchase, and the in-house rigging are the three reasons we keep this hull on the floor.

Drop into 11 White Oak Drive East to see one on the floor. Casey can walk you through Mercury powering options on the sales floor.

Reyco's been on White Oak Drive for more than 60 years. The dealership has grown across boats, motors, and lawn-and-snow equipment, but the core of what we do hasn't changed — we sell what we'd put on our own gear, we service what we sell on our own bench, and we back the warranty paperwork in-house so the customer doesn't have to chase it. That's the part of an authorised-dealer purchase that doesn't show up on the price tag, and it's the reason most customers in Sault Ste. Marie don't think twice about where they go for marine and outdoor power equipment. We've earned that trust one boat, one motor, and one season at a time. The relationship is meant to run for years — not just the duration of a sale. When the unit needs attention, you stop in or you call, and someone on the team who knows you answers. That's the dealer side that customers tell us they value most.

Name: 2022 Mercury 9.9 HP FourStroke Outboard Motor
Short description: Mercury's 9.9 hp FourStroke sized as small kicker / tiller for a 14-foot Lund or aluminum jon. Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer — we register the warranty in-house and service it on our bench at White Oak Drive.
Description:
For boats running the St. Marys River, the 2022 Mercury 9.9 hp in small kicker / tiller configuration is one of the motors Reyco recommends most. The reason is simple: this is freshwater Mercury knows well — the corrosion package, the cold-start fuel management, and the run-quality at lower water temperatures are all dialled for the conditions our customers actually fish.

On a 14-foot Lund or aluminum jon, this motor lands in the operating sweet spot. Underpowered hulls plow; overpowered hulls stress the transom. The 9.9 hp puts the rig where its designer intended — that's the reason small kicker / tiller is the most-recommended class for this boat size.

As an authorised Mercury dealer, Reyco handles warranty registration in-house, services on our own bench, and stocks the consumables on the parts counter. Lee on the service bench knows the current Mercury internals; Lynn at the parts counter and Ron behind it pull the spark plugs, lower-unit oil, and props off the shelf.

Stop by the showroom and Lee on the service bench can walk you through rigging.

What separates a dealer from a marketplace listing is what happens after the sale. At Reyco, that means warranty registration handled the day you take delivery, break-in or first-service slots booked in our own shop, and a parts counter that already has the consumables for this unit on the shelf. We've been doing the work in Sault Ste. Marie for more than 60 years — long enough to have seen multiple generations of Mercury, Princecraft, Minn Kota, Cannon, and Humminbird product redesigns roll through the same showroom. The relationships we've built with the brand reps, the technical support lines, and the dealer networks are part of what we bring to every sale. That's the value of buying from a long-tenured authorised dealer. When something needs warranty attention, we know who to call and we get an answer the same day in most cases. That's not something a box-store competitor can match.

Meta: _product_expert_review: I run a marine business in a town where the customer doesn't have a backup if the kicker dies — that's why I stock Mercury over the alternatives, and that's why I tell customers the Mercury 9.9 FourStroke is the unit I'd put on my own boat for small kicker / tiller use. On a 14-foot Lund or aluminum jon, this motor sits in its sweet spot; on the bench, Lee can service it without sending parts away. I've sold enough of them around the St. Marys River to know the failure modes, and there aren't many. The corrosion package handles freshwater dock-life well — that's a real consideration for customers who keep the boat in slip on Lake Superior. Come down to the showroom and Lee or I can answer rigging questions. We're not the cheapest dealer you'll find, and we're not trying to be — we're the dealer that's still here when you need help five seasons in.

Name: 2026 Mercury 40 HP FourStroke Outboard Motor
Short description: Mercury's 40 hp FourStroke sized as mid-power main for a 16- to 18-foot aluminum runabout or fishing boat. Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer — we register the warranty in-house and service it on our bench at White Oak Drive.
Description:
Customers shopping a Mercury 40 hp ask the same question — what's the 2026 build going to do over five-plus seasons? For mid-power main power on a 16- to 18-foot aluminum runabout or fishing boat, the FourStroke engineering is the answer: refined fuel management, dependable cold-start, and the corrosion package Mercury ships across the modern range.

Casey's read on this output: it's the motor that does the most for the most boats in our customer base. The weekend tournament guy is the buyer it serves best. The motor is sized for the hull, the fuel burn is reasonable, and the cold-start is what Mercury customers in Whitefish Bay expect.

After the sale: we handle the warranty paperwork, service it on our own bench, and keep the consumables in stock. Lee in the service department, Lynn managing the parts counter, Ron pulling the accessory orders. That's the dealer side of the deal — the part you don't get when you buy a motor from a box-store or off a marketplace listing.

Speak to Ron at the parts counter for accessories that pair with this unit.

There's a reason customers in Northern Ontario keep coming back to Reyco for marine purchases — the in-house service relationship is the part that matters when the boat is out on water, the lake is cold, and the customer needs help. Lee in the service department, Lynn managing the parts counter, Ron pulling accessories, Casey, Aaron, and Kory on the sales floor — that's the team that backs the unit you buy from us. We see most boats back in for shoulder-season service every year, and that's how the relationship is meant to run. The customer who buys here doesn't end up calling a 1-800 line when something needs attention; they call us, and the call gets answered by someone who's already been on the boat. That's the kind of dealer-customer continuity that takes 60-plus years on a single street to build, and it's not something we take for granted.
