Name: 2025 Mercury 20 HP FourStroke Tiller Manual Start Outboard Motor
Short description: Mercury's 20 hp FourStroke sized as mid-range tiller for a 14- to 16-foot aluminum. Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer — we register the warranty in-house and service it on our bench at White Oak Drive.
Description:
Of all the Mercury FourStroke outboards in the mid-range tiller class on our floor right now, the 2025 20 hp is the unit we keep stocked longest because it walks out the door fastest. Current engineering, factory-authorised through Reyco.

Sized for a 14- to 16-foot aluminum, recommended for the cottage owner who needs more push, and built to handle a bracing cold-front day on the water the way Mercury's FourStroke line has been refined to. It's a motor we sell to first-time outboard buyers and to customers on their fifth Mercury — and the conversation is the same either way.

From Reyco, the package includes warranty registration, in-house service from Lee in the service department, and the parts-counter coverage Lynn and Ron run on Mercury consumables. We register the motor before it leaves the lot, we book the break-in service before the customer asks, and we see the boat back in the shoulder season for end-of-year winterization.

Book a service slot with Lee in the service department before next launch.

Reyco runs as an authorised dealer for the brands we carry because the warranty backing and the in-house service are what make the long-term ownership work. We register the warranty before the unit leaves the lot. We book the break-in service before the customer asks. We carry the consumables on the parts counter so when something needs replacing, the customer walks out with the part the same day. That's the dealer-side value, and it's the reason customers in Northern Ontario keep buying from us across decades — not just one purchase. Casey, Aaron, and Kory on the sales floor can talk you through any of this; Lynn and Ron handle the parts side; Lee handles service. It's a small team that knows the customer, and we like it that way. We've watched generations of families buy boats here — fathers, sons, and now grandkids — and that's the kind of relationship a 60-plus-year dealership earns.

Name: 2024 Mercury 8 HP FourStroke Outboard Motor
Short description: Mercury's 8 hp FourStroke sized as small kicker / tiller for a 14-foot Lund or aluminum jon. Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer — we register the warranty in-house and service it on our bench at White Oak Drive.
Description:
The 2024 Mercury 8 hp FourStroke is the kind of motor you can pull off the rack, set on the transom of a 14-foot Lund or aluminum jon, and be fishing inside of five minutes. That's the use-case Reyco sees this output turn up for most often — primary motor for the back-lake walleye angler, headed somewhere out on the Trans-Canada cottage country.

The FourStroke 8 is sized as small kicker / tiller-class power. What that means in plain English: it'll push the hulls it's meant for at the speeds those hulls are designed to run, without the engine working overtime. Mercury's FourStroke line has been refined long enough that the cold-start behaviour is what we'd expect — the boat starts on the first or second pull, even on a cold-water October morning.

Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer. When you buy this motor from us, we register the warranty in-house and Lee in the service department is the same tech who'll see it again at break-in service or end-of-season winterization. We carry the spark plugs, lower-unit oil, propellers and pull-cord assemblies for the FourStroke and Pro XS lines on the parts counter — Lynn manages the parts counter and Ron knows the Mercury accessory line cold.

Casey carries Mercury because the warranty backs the customer in a way that matters when the dealer is the one a buyer trusts to make it right. Speak to Ron at the parts counter for accessories that pair with this unit.

Reyco's been on White Oak Drive for more than 60 years. The dealership has grown across boats, motors, and lawn-and-snow equipment, but the core of what we do hasn't changed — we sell what we'd put on our own gear, we service what we sell on our own bench, and we back the warranty paperwork in-house so the customer doesn't have to chase it. That's the part of an authorised-dealer purchase that doesn't show up on the price tag, and it's the reason most customers in Sault Ste. Marie don't think twice about where they go for marine and outdoor power equipment. We've earned that trust one boat, one motor, and one season at a time. The relationship is meant to run for years — not just the duration of a sale. When the unit needs attention, you stop in or you call, and someone on the team who knows you answers. That's the dealer side that customers tell us they value most.

Name: 2024 Mercury 25 HP FourStroke Tiller Electric Start EFI Outboard Motor
Short description: Mercury's 25 hp FourStroke sized as mid-range tiller for a 14- to 16-foot aluminum. Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer — we register the warranty in-house and service it on our bench at White Oak Drive.
Description:
For the cottage owner who needs more push, the 2024 Mercury 25 hp FourStroke is the unit we'd hand you the keys to first. The mid-range tiller output is designed for a 14- to 16-foot aluminum — exactly the hull most 2024 buyers are putting it on.

There's nothing exotic about this motor. It's Mercury's well-proven FourStroke engineering: reliable cold-water start, predictable fuel burn, and the kind of run-quality that means the boat goes the distance you need it to without drama. We've sold enough 25 hp Mercurys around Bruce Mines to know the failure modes — and there aren't many.

What you get when you buy from Reyco: factory-authorised registration, service from Lee on the service bench (engines, boats, ATV — he covers it), and a parts counter with the consumables for this motor already stocked. Lynn manages the parts shelf, Ron handles the Mercury accessory pulls. If the boat needs a winterization at end of season, we book it in and turn it around in the shoulder-season window before the spring rush.

Book a service slot with Lee in the service department before next launch.

What separates a dealer from a marketplace listing is what happens after the sale. At Reyco, that means warranty registration handled the day you take delivery, break-in or first-service slots booked in our own shop, and a parts counter that already has the consumables for this unit on the shelf. We've been doing the work in Sault Ste. Marie for more than 60 years — long enough to have seen multiple generations of Mercury, Princecraft, Minn Kota, Cannon, and Humminbird product redesigns roll through the same showroom. The relationships we've built with the brand reps, the technical support lines, and the dealer networks are part of what we bring to every sale. That's the value of buying from a long-tenured authorised dealer. When something needs warranty attention, we know who to call and we get an answer the same day in most cases. That's not something a box-store competitor can match.

Name: 2026 Mercury 90 HP FourStroke 25-inch Shaft Command Thrust Outboard Motor
Short description: Mercury's 90 hp FourStroke sized as mid-large main for an 18- to 20-foot aluminum or fibreglass. Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer — we register the warranty in-house and service it on our bench at White Oak Drive.
Description:
On Northern Ontario, the Mercury 90 hp FourStroke is one of the motors Reyco carries because the difference between a 2026 build that starts cold and one that doesn't is the difference between fishing and hauling the boat home. That's the calculus when you run a marine business in a town where the customer doesn't have a backup if the kicker dies.

This is mid-large main-class power, sized for an 18- to 20-foot aluminum or fibreglass. The FourStroke designation means it's part of Mercury's modern four-stroke product line — quieter than a two-stroke, runs unleaded out of any can in the shed, and doesn't smoke up the cottage. For the year-round Great Lakes troller, that matters more than spec-sheet horsepower numbers.

Reyco registers the warranty in-house and we service every Mercury we sell on our own bench. Lee in the service department handles the marine work, and the parts counter — Lynn and Ron — keeps the FourStroke consumables on the shelf so when you need a plug or a prop pin, you walk out with it.

Drop into 11 White Oak Drive East to see one on the floor. Casey, Aaron, or Kory on the sales floor can walk you through specifics.

There's a reason customers in Northern Ontario keep coming back to Reyco for marine purchases — the in-house service relationship is the part that matters when the boat is out on water, the lake is cold, and the customer needs help. Lee in the service department, Lynn managing the parts counter, Ron pulling accessories, Casey, Aaron, and Kory on the sales floor — that's the team that backs the unit you buy from us. We see most boats back in for shoulder-season service every year, and that's how the relationship is meant to run. The customer who buys here doesn't end up calling a 1-800 line when something needs attention; they call us, and the call gets answered by someone who's already been on the boat. That's the kind of dealer-customer continuity that takes 60-plus years on a single street to build, and it's not something we take for granted.

On the back lakes off the Trans-Canada, the 15 hp class of Mercury outboard is the one that does the most work for the most boats. This 2024 FourStroke build is sized as small kicker / tiller power for a 14-foot Lund or aluminum jon — the configuration we see in our customer fleet more than any other.

What you're getting: the 2024 FourStroke build, with Mercury's current cold-start, electronic-fuel-management, and corrosion-protection package as standard. The motor doesn't care whether you're the back-lake walleye angler or the lighter-tinny owner — it'll do the same job either way.

Reyco's been an authorised Mercury dealer for the better part of three generations of motor design. That means: we register the warranty in-house, we service every motor we sell on our own bench, and Lee in the service department knows the FourStroke internals as well as anyone in the back lakes off the Trans-Canada. Parts? Lynn manages the counter; Ron handles the accessory pulls. Most consumables for this motor are already on our shelves.

Speak to Ron at the parts counter for accessories that pair with this unit.

Reyco's been on White Oak Drive for more than 60 years. The dealership has grown across boats, motors, and lawn-and-snow equipment, but the core of what we do hasn't changed — we sell what we'd put on our own gear, we service what we sell on our own bench, and we back the warranty paperwork in-house so the customer doesn't have to chase it. That's the part of an authorised-dealer purchase that doesn't show up on the price tag, and it's the reason most customers in Sault Ste. Marie don't think twice about where they go for marine and outdoor power equipment. We've earned that trust one boat, one motor, and one season at a time. The relationship is meant to run for years — not just the duration of a sale. When the unit needs attention, you stop in or you call, and someone on the team who knows you answers. That's the dealer side that customers tell us they value most.

Description:
When a customer walks in asking what 2023 Mercury they should put on a 16- to 18-foot aluminum runabout or fishing boat, the 40 hp in mid-power main configuration is the answer Casey gives most often in this output range. The FourStroke build fits the hull and the use-case cleanly.

It comes down to fit. Underpowered, the boat plows; overpowered, the rig stresses the transom and burns fuel for no return. The 40 hp lands in the operating zone the hull was designed for — that's why we recommend it for the family fishing-boat owner on a typical Reyco-spec rig.

On the back end: we're an authorised Mercury dealer, which means warranty registration and factory-authorised service in-house. Lee runs the service bench, Lynn manages the parts counter, Ron handles the Mercury accessory line. We carry the consumables for the FourStroke engines on the shelf so if you stop in for a plug or a prop pin, you walk out with it the same day.

Book a service slot with Lee in the service department before next launch.

What separates a dealer from a marketplace listing is what happens after the sale. At Reyco, that means warranty registration handled the day you take delivery, break-in or first-service slots booked in our own shop, and a parts counter that already has the consumables for this unit on the shelf. We've been doing the work in Sault Ste. Marie for more than 60 years — long enough to have seen multiple generations of Mercury, Princecraft, Minn Kota, Cannon, and Humminbird product redesigns roll through the same showroom. The relationships we've built with the brand reps, the technical support lines, and the dealer networks are part of what we bring to every sale. That's the value of buying from a long-tenured authorised dealer. When something needs warranty attention, we know who to call and we get an answer the same day in most cases. That's not something a box-store competitor can match.

Short description: Mercury's 60 hp FourStroke sized as mid-power main for a 16- to 18-foot aluminum runabout or fishing boat. Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer — we register the warranty in-house and service it on our bench at White Oak Drive.
Description:
60 hp. 2025 model year. FourStroke. That's the bare spec sheet — and on the showroom floor, this motor is the mid-power main option Reyco recommends first to the family fishing-boat owner on a 16- to 18-foot aluminum runabout or fishing boat.

Past the spec sheet, what matters is how the motor behaves on the water. Mercury's FourStroke line is engineered for cold-start reliability — meaningful in Lake Superior where a bracing cold-front day on the water is half the season. The fuel system, the alternator output (where applicable), and the corrosion package are all current-generation Mercury build.

What we add as a dealer: factory-authorised warranty registration, in-house service from Lee in the service department (he covers engines, boats, ATV — broad bench), and a parts counter that keeps the FourStroke and Pro XS consumables in stock. Lynn manages the counter; Ron and the rest of the parts team know which spark plug, which lower-unit oil, which prop fits which rigging.

Drop into 11 White Oak Drive East to see one on the floor.

There's a reason customers in Northern Ontario keep coming back to Reyco for marine purchases — the in-house service relationship is the part that matters when the boat is out on water, the lake is cold, and the customer needs help. Name: 2023 Mercury 15 HP FourStroke 20-inch Shaft Tiller Electric Start (Unit 1598) Outboard Motor
Short description: Mercury's 15 hp FourStroke sized as small kicker / tiller for a 14-foot Lund or aluminum jon. Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer — we register the warranty in-house and service it on our bench at White Oak Drive.
Description:
Most customers who buy a 2023 Mercury 15 hp from Reyco see Lee in the service department within the first year — not for repair, but for the break-in service the FourStroke line benefits from. That's the shape of the relationship around an authorised-dealer purchase, and it's a good sign for the buyer.

This is small kicker / tiller-class power, sized for a 14-foot Lund or aluminum jon. The use case is the back-lake walleye angler on water like the St. Marys River — the kind of running where the motor earns its keep over a multi-year horizon.

Reyco's been a Mercury dealer long enough that we've seen the 15 hp class of outboard go through three Mercury product redesigns. The current 2023 build is in our experience the most refined of those generations — quieter, smoother fuel curve, better cold-start. Past the marketing, it's a motor we trust to leave the lot and start every spring.

Stop by the showroom and Lee on the service bench can walk you through rigging.

Reyco runs as an authorised dealer for the brands we carry because the warranty backing and the in-house service are what make the long-term ownership work. We register the warranty before the unit leaves the lot. We book the break-in service before the customer asks. We carry the consumables on the parts counter so when something needs replacing, the customer walks out with the part the same day. That's the dealer-side value, and it's the reason customers in Northern Ontario keep buying from us across decades — not just one purchase. Casey, Aaron, and Kory on the sales floor can talk you through any of this; Lynn and Ron handle the parts side; Lee handles service. It's a small team that knows the customer, and we like it that way. We've watched generations of families buy boats here — fathers, sons, and now grandkids — and that's the kind of relationship a 60-plus-year dealership earns.

This 2025 Mercury 50 hp is current-generation FourStroke engineering, factory-authorised through Reyco. Mid-power main power for a 16- to 18-foot aluminum runabout or fishing boat, and that's the configuration we sell more than any other in this output range.

The FourStroke line — when applicable — is Mercury's modern four-stroke architecture. EFI on the larger motors, carburetted on the smaller portables, with corrosion-protection across the range that matters when the boat lives at a dock on Lake Superior or anywhere with hard freshwater. The motor handles an ice-out April launch predictably; the fuel curve doesn't surprise you.

From Reyco, you also get the dealer side of the package. Lee in the service department handles the marine bench. Lynn manages the parts counter. Ron, our parts specialist, knows the Mercury accessory catalogue cold. Casey, Aaron, and Kory on the sales floor can walk you through fit on your specific hull.

Speak to Ron at the parts counter for accessories that pair with this unit.

Reyco's been on White Oak Drive for more than 60 years. The dealership has grown across boats, motors, and lawn-and-snow equipment, but the core of what we do hasn't changed — we sell what we'd put on our own gear, we service what we sell on our own bench, and we back the warranty paperwork in-house so the customer doesn't have to chase it. That's the part of an authorised-dealer purchase that doesn't show up on the price tag, and it's the reason most customers in Sault Ste. Marie don't think twice about where they go for marine and outdoor power equipment. We've earned that trust one boat, one motor, and one season at a time. The relationship is meant to run for years — not just the duration of a sale. When the unit needs attention, you stop in or you call, and someone on the team who knows you answers. That's the dealer side that customers tell us they value most.

Name: 2014 Mercury 50 HP FourStroke Outboard Motor
Short description: New old stock 2014 Mercury 50 hp FourStroke — uncrated and unused, full Mercury warranty starts on registration. Sized as mid-power main for a 16- to 18-foot aluminum runabout or fishing boat. Stocked at Reyco Marine in Sault Ste. Marie.
Description:
For boats running Algoma, the 2014 Mercury 50 hp in mid-power main configuration is one of the motors Reyco recommends most. The reason is simple: this is freshwater Mercury knows well — the corrosion package, the cold-start fuel management, and the run-quality at lower water temperatures are all dialled for the conditions our customers actually fish. One note on this particular unit: it's 2014 model-year stock that's been on our floor crated, unused — a true new-old-stock motor. Mercury warranty starts the day we register it for the buyer.

On a 16- to 18-foot aluminum runabout or fishing boat, this motor lands in the operating sweet spot. Underpowered hulls plow; overpowered hulls stress the transom. The 50 hp puts the rig where its designer intended — that's the reason mid-power main is the most-recommended class for this boat size.

As an authorised Mercury dealer, Reyco handles warranty registration in-house, services on our own bench, and stocks the consumables on the parts counter. Lee on the service bench knows the current Mercury internals; Lynn at the parts counter and Ron behind it pull the spark plugs, lower-unit oil, and props off the shelf.

Book a service slot with Lee in the service department before next launch.

What separates a dealer from a marketplace listing is what happens after the sale. At Reyco, that means warranty registration handled the day you take delivery, break-in or first-service slots booked in our own shop, and a parts counter that already has the consumables for this unit on the shelf. We've been doing the work in Sault Ste. Marie for more than 60 years — long enough to have seen multiple generations of Mercury, Princecraft, Minn Kota, Cannon, and Humminbird product redesigns roll through the same showroom. The relationships we've built with the brand reps, the technical support lines, and the dealer networks are part of what we bring to every sale. That's the value of buying from a long-tenured authorised dealer. When something needs warranty attention, we know who to call and we get an answer the same day in most cases. That's not something a box-store competitor can match.

Customers shopping a Mercury 15 hp ask the same question — what's the 2022 build going to do over five-plus seasons? For small kicker / tiller power on a 14-foot Lund or aluminum jon, the FourStroke engineering is the answer: refined fuel management, dependable cold-start, and the corrosion package Mercury ships across the modern range.

Casey's read on this output: it's the motor that does the most for the most boats in our customer base. The cottage daily-driver is the buyer it serves best. The motor is sized for the hull, the fuel burn is reasonable, and the cold-start is what Mercury customers in the Trans-Canada cottage country expect.

After the sale: we handle the warranty paperwork, service it on our own bench, and keep the consumables in stock. Lee in the service department, Lynn managing the parts counter, Ron pulling the accessory orders. That's the dealer side of the deal — the part you don't get when you buy a motor from a box-store or off a marketplace listing.

Drop into 11 White Oak Drive East to see one on the floor.

There's a reason customers in Northern Ontario keep coming back to Reyco for marine purchases — the in-house service relationship is the part that matters when the boat is out on water, the lake is cold, and the customer needs help. Lee in the service department, Lynn managing the parts counter, Ron pulling accessories, Casey, Aaron, and Kory on the sales floor — that's the team that backs the unit you buy from us. We see most boats back in for shoulder-season service every year, and that's how the relationship is meant to run. The customer who buys here doesn't end up calling a 1-800 line when something needs attention; they call us, and the call gets answered by someone who's already been on the boat. That's the kind of dealer-customer continuity that takes 60-plus years on a single street to build, and it's not something we take for granted.

Description:
Of all the Mercury FourStroke outboards in the mid-power main class on our floor right now, the 2023 60 hp is the unit we keep stocked longest because it walks out the door fastest. Current engineering, factory-authorised through Reyco.

Sized for a 16- to 18-foot aluminum runabout or fishing boat, recommended for the family fishing-boat owner, and built to handle a chilly first-launch of the season the way Mercury's FourStroke line has been refined to. It's a motor we sell to first-time outboard buyers and to customers on their fifth Mercury — and the conversation is the same either way.

From Reyco, the package includes warranty registration, in-house service from Lee in the service department, and the parts-counter coverage Lynn and Ron run on Mercury consumables. We register the motor before it leaves the lot, we book the break-in service before the customer asks, and we see the boat back in the shoulder season for end-of-year winterization.

Stop by the showroom and Lee on the service bench can walk you through rigging.

Reyco runs as an authorised dealer for the brands we carry because the warranty backing and the in-house service are what make the long-term ownership work. We register the warranty before the unit leaves the lot. We book the break-in service before the customer asks. We carry the consumables on the parts counter so when something needs replacing, the customer walks out with the part the same day. That's the dealer-side value, and it's the reason customers in Northern Ontario keep buying from us across decades — not just one purchase. Casey, Aaron, and Kory on the sales floor can talk you through any of this; Lynn and Ron handle the parts side; Lee handles service. It's a small team that knows the customer, and we like it that way. We've watched generations of families buy boats here — fathers, sons, and now grandkids — and that's the kind of relationship a 60-plus-year dealership earns.

Name: 2026 Mercury 50 HP FourStroke Outboard Motor
Short description: Mercury's 50 hp FourStroke sized as mid-power main for a 16- to 18-foot aluminum runabout or fishing boat. Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer — we register the warranty in-house and service it on our bench at White Oak Drive.
Description:
The 2026 Mercury 50 hp FourStroke is the kind of motor you can pull off the rack, set on the transom of a 16- to 18-foot aluminum runabout or fishing boat, and be fishing inside of five minutes. That's the use-case Reyco sees this output turn up for most often — primary motor for the family fishing-boat owner, headed somewhere out on Northern Ontario.

The FourStroke 50 is sized as mid-power main-class power. What that means in plain English: it'll push the hulls it's meant for at the speeds those hulls are designed to run, without the engine working overtime. Mercury's FourStroke line has been refined long enough that the cold-start behaviour is what we'd expect — the boat starts on the first or second pull, even on an early-spring trolling run.

Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer. When you buy this motor from us, we register the warranty in-house and Lee in the service department is the same tech who'll see it again at break-in service or end-of-season winterization. We carry the spark plugs, lower-unit oil, propellers and pull-cord assemblies for the FourStroke and Pro XS lines on the parts counter — Lynn manages the parts counter and Ron knows the Mercury accessory line cold.

Casey carries Mercury because the warranty backs the customer in a way that matters when the dealer is the one a buyer trusts to make it right. Drop into 11 White Oak Drive East to see one on the floor.

Reyco's been on White Oak Drive for more than 60 years. The dealership has grown across boats, motors, and lawn-and-snow equipment, but the core of what we do hasn't changed — we sell what we'd put on our own gear, we service what we sell on our own bench, and we back the warranty paperwork in-house so the customer doesn't have to chase it. That's the part of an authorised-dealer purchase that doesn't show up on the price tag, and it's the reason most customers in Sault Ste. Marie don't think twice about where they go for marine and outdoor power equipment. We've earned that trust one boat, one motor, and one season at a time. The relationship is meant to run for years — not just the duration of a sale. When the unit needs attention, you stop in or you call, and someone on the team who knows you answers. That's the dealer side that customers tell us they value most.

Name: 2025 Mercury 40 HP FourStroke Outboard Motor
Short description: Mercury's 40 hp FourStroke sized as mid-power main for a 16- to 18-foot aluminum runabout or fishing boat. Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer — we register the warranty in-house and service it on our bench at White Oak Drive.
Description:
For the family fishing-boat owner, the 2025 Mercury 40 hp FourStroke is the unit we'd hand you the keys to first. The mid-power main output is designed for a 16- to 18-foot aluminum runabout or fishing boat — exactly the hull most 2025 buyers are putting it on.

There's nothing exotic about this motor. It's Mercury's well-proven FourStroke engineering: reliable cold-water start, predictable fuel burn, and the kind of run-quality that means the boat goes the distance you need it to without drama. We've sold enough 40 hp Mercurys around the Sault to know the failure modes — and there aren't many.

What you get when you buy from Reyco: factory-authorised registration, service from Lee on the service bench (engines, boats, ATV — he covers it), and a parts counter with the consumables for this motor already stocked. Lynn manages the parts shelf, Ron handles the Mercury accessory pulls. If the boat needs a winterization at end of season, we book it in and turn it around in the shoulder-season window before the spring rush.

Stop by the showroom and Lee on the service bench can walk you through rigging.

What separates a dealer from a marketplace listing is what happens after the sale. At Reyco, that means warranty registration handled the day you take delivery, break-in or first-service slots booked in our own shop, and a parts counter that already has the consumables for this unit on the shelf. We've been doing the work in Sault Ste. Marie for more than 60 years — long enough to have seen multiple generations of Mercury, Princecraft, Minn Kota, Cannon, and Humminbird product redesigns roll through the same showroom. The relationships we've built with the brand reps, the technical support lines, and the dealer networks are part of what we bring to every sale. That's the value of buying from a long-tenured authorised dealer. When something needs warranty attention, we know who to call and we get an answer the same day in most cases. That's not something a box-store competitor can match.

Name: 2026 Mercury 150 HP FourStroke Outboard Motor
Short description: Mercury's 150 hp FourStroke sized as offshore-class main for a 20-foot-plus glass or aluminum. Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer — we register the warranty in-house and service it on our bench at White Oak Drive.
Description:
On the back lakes off the Trans-Canada, the Mercury 150 hp FourStroke is one of the motors Reyco carries because the difference between a 2026 build that starts cold and one that doesn't is the difference between fishing and hauling the boat home. That's the calculus when you run a marine business in a town where the customer doesn't have a backup if the kicker dies.

This is offshore-class main-class power, sized for a 20-foot-plus glass or aluminum. The FourStroke designation means it's part of Mercury's modern four-stroke product line — quieter than a two-stroke, runs unleaded out of any can in the shed, and doesn't smoke up the cottage. For the offshore charter operator, that matters more than spec-sheet horsepower numbers.

Reyco registers the warranty in-house and we service every Mercury we sell on our own bench. Lee in the service department handles the marine work, and the parts counter — Lynn and Ron — keeps the FourStroke consumables on the shelf so when you need a plug or a prop pin, you walk out with it.

Speak to Ron at the parts counter for accessories that pair with this unit. Casey, Aaron, or Kory on the sales floor can walk you through specifics.

There's a reason customers in Northern Ontario keep coming back to Reyco for marine purchases — the in-house service relationship is the part that matters when the boat is out on water, the lake is cold, and the customer needs help. Lee in the service department, Lynn managing the parts counter, Ron pulling accessories, Casey, Aaron, and Kory on the sales floor — that's the team that backs the unit you buy from us. We see most boats back in for shoulder-season service every year, and that's how the relationship is meant to run. The customer who buys here doesn't end up calling a 1-800 line when something needs attention; they call us, and the call gets answered by someone who's already been on the boat. That's the kind of dealer-customer continuity that takes 60-plus years on a single street to build, and it's not something we take for granted.

Name: 2021 Mercury 20 HP FourStroke Outboard Motor
Short description: Mercury's 20 hp FourStroke sized as mid-range tiller for a 14- to 16-foot aluminum. Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer — we register the warranty in-house and service it on our bench at White Oak Drive.
Description:
The 20 hp Mercury sits in the mid-range tiller sweet spot — the 2021 build is the size of motor that does the most for the most customers in our base. We move more 20-class Mercurys through this dealership than most other single outputs in the lineup, and there's a reason: it fits a 14- to 16-foot aluminum cleanly without overpowering or underpowering the rig.

What's behind the spec sheet: this is the 2021 model-year unit, in current Mercury build. The FourStroke / Pro XS engineering — depending on the trim — covers the cold-start reliability, the fuel-management refinement, and the run-quality that customers expect from Mercury. We don't fit these to the wrong hull; if you tell us what you're running, Casey will tell you whether this is the right output or whether you should look at the next-step-up unit on the floor.

On the service side: Lee in the service department handles the marine bench, the parts counter keeps the consumables in stock, and we register the warranty before the motor leaves the lot. That's the difference between buying from an authorised dealer and buying online.

Book a service slot with Lee in the service department before next launch.

Reyco runs as an authorised dealer for the brands we carry because the warranty backing and the in-house service are what make the long-term ownership work. We register the warranty before the unit leaves the lot. We book the break-in service before the customer asks. We carry the consumables on the parts counter so when something needs replacing, the customer walks out with the part the same day. That's the dealer-side value, and it's the reason customers in Northern Ontario keep buying from us across decades — not just one purchase. Casey, Aaron, and Kory on the sales floor can talk you through any of this; Lynn and Ron handle the parts side; Lee handles service. It's a small team that knows the customer, and we like it that way. We've watched generations of families buy boats here — fathers, sons, and now grandkids — and that's the kind of relationship a 60-plus-year dealership earns.

We're not the cheapest dealer you'll find, and we're not trying to be — we're the dealer that's still here when you need help five seasons in.

Short description: Mercury's 25 hp FourStroke sized as mid-range tiller for a 14- to 16-foot aluminum. Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer — we register the warranty in-house and service it on our bench at White Oak Drive.
Description:
On Lake Superior, the 25 hp class of Mercury outboard is the one that does the most work for the most boats. This 2025 FourStroke build is sized as mid-range tiller power for a 14- to 16-foot aluminum — the configuration we see in our customer fleet more than any other.

What you're getting: the 2025 FourStroke build, with Mercury's current cold-start, electronic-fuel-management, and corrosion-protection package as standard. The motor doesn't care whether you're the cottage owner who needs more push or the small fishing-camp operator — it'll do the same job either way.

Reyco's been an authorised Mercury dealer for the better part of three generations of motor design. That means: we register the warranty in-house, we service every motor we sell on our own bench, and Lee in the service department knows the FourStroke internals as well as anyone in Lake Superior. Parts? Lynn manages the counter; Ron handles the accessory pulls. Most consumables for this motor are already on our shelves.

Drop into 11 White Oak Drive East to see one on the floor.

Reyco's been on White Oak Drive for more than 60 years. The dealership has grown across boats, motors, and lawn-and-snow equipment, but the core of what we do hasn't changed — we sell what we'd put on our own gear, we service what we sell on our own bench, and we back the warranty paperwork in-house so the customer doesn't have to chase it. That's the part of an authorised-dealer purchase that doesn't show up on the price tag, and it's the reason most customers in Sault Ste. Marie don't think twice about where they go for marine and outdoor power equipment. We've earned that trust one boat, one motor, and one season at a time. The relationship is meant to run for years — not just the duration of a sale. When the unit needs attention, you stop in or you call, and someone on the team who knows you answers. That's the dealer side that customers tell us they value most.

Name: 2023 Mercury 15 HP FourStroke 20-inch Shaft Tiller Electric Start (Unit 5495) Outboard Motor
Short description: Mercury's 15 hp FourStroke sized as small kicker / tiller for a 14-foot Lund or aluminum jon. Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer — we register the warranty in-house and service it on our bench at White Oak Drive.
Description:
When a customer walks in asking what 2023 Mercury they should put on a 14-foot Lund or aluminum jon, the 15 hp in small kicker / tiller configuration is the answer Casey gives most often in this output range. The FourStroke build fits the hull and the use-case cleanly.

It comes down to fit. Underpowered, the boat plows; overpowered, the rig stresses the transom and burns fuel for no return. The 15 hp lands in the operating zone the hull was designed for — that's why we recommend it for the back-lake walleye angler on a typical Reyco-spec rig.

On the back end: we're an authorised Mercury dealer, which means warranty registration and factory-authorised service in-house. Lee runs the service bench, Lynn manages the parts counter, Ron handles the Mercury accessory line. We carry the consumables for the FourStroke engines on the shelf so if you stop in for a plug or a prop pin, you walk out with it the same day.

Stop by the showroom and Lee on the service bench can walk you through rigging.

What separates a dealer from a marketplace listing is what happens after the sale. At Reyco, that means warranty registration handled the day you take delivery, break-in or first-service slots booked in our own shop, and a parts counter that already has the consumables for this unit on the shelf. We've been doing the work in Sault Ste. Marie for more than 60 years — long enough to have seen multiple generations of Mercury, Princecraft, Minn Kota, Cannon, and Humminbird product redesigns roll through the same showroom. The relationships we've built with the brand reps, the technical support lines, and the dealer networks are part of what we bring to every sale. That's the value of buying from a long-tenured authorised dealer. When something needs warranty attention, we know who to call and we get an answer the same day in most cases. That's not something a box-store competitor can match.

Name: 2022 Mercury 60 HP FourStroke Outboard Motor
Short description: Mercury's 60 hp FourStroke sized as mid-power main for a 16- to 18-foot aluminum runabout or fishing boat. Reyco is an authorised Mercury dealer — we register the warranty in-house and service it on our bench at White Oak Drive.
Description:
60 hp. 2022 model year. FourStroke. That's the bare spec sheet — and on the showroom floor, this motor is the mid-power main option Reyco recommends first to the family fishing-boat owner on a 16- to 18-foot aluminum runabout or fishing boat.

Past the spec sheet, what matters is how the motor behaves on the water. Mercury's FourStroke line is engineered for cold-start reliability — meaningful in Whitefish Bay where a chilly first-launch of the season is half the season. The fuel system, the alternator output (where applicable), and the corrosion package are all current-generation Mercury build.

What we add as a dealer: factory-authorised warranty registration, in-house service from Lee in the service department (he covers engines, boats, ATV — broad bench), and a parts counter that keeps the FourStroke and Pro XS consumables in stock. Lynn manages the counter; Ron and the rest of the parts team know which spark plug, which lower-unit oil, which prop fits which rigging.

Speak to Ron at the parts counter for accessories that pair with this unit.

There's a reason customers in Northern Ontario keep coming back to Reyco for marine purchases — the in-house service relationship is the part that matters when the boat is out on water, the lake is cold, and the customer needs help. Lee in the service department, Lynn managing the parts counter, Ron pulling accessories, Casey, Aaron, and Kory on the sales floor — that's the team that backs the unit you buy from us. We see most boats back in for shoulder-season service every year, and that's how the relationship is meant to run. The customer who buys here doesn't end up calling a 1-800 line when something needs attention; they call us, and the call gets answered by someone who's already been on the boat. That's the kind of dealer-customer continuity that takes 60-plus years on a single street to build, and it's not something we take for granted.

Name: 2023 Princecraft Sportfisher 21-ft 2S Fishing Edition Pontoon
Short description: 2023 Princecraft Sportfisher 21-ft pontoon — Reyco is an authorised Princecraft dealer in Sault Ste. Marie. Available off the lot at White Oak Drive. Talk to Casey about fit on your lake.
Description:
The 2023 Princecraft Sportfisher 21-ft pontoon is the kind of boat the cottage country around Algoma runs on summer weekends — built for relaxed cruising, family swims, sundown trolling, and the back-deck dinner that follows. Princecraft is a Canadian-built brand, and the Sportfisher sits in the part of the lineup we move most often through Reyco.

Casey carries Princecraft because the Canadian build matters in Algoma. The aluminum construction, the fit-and-finish on the deck and rails, and the Princecraft after-sales network are all reasons we've stocked the brand longer than most lineups on the floor. We're an authorised Princecraft dealer — the boat is registered, rigged, and water-tested through our service department before it leaves the lot.

Lee in the service department handles the rigging and outboard fit (we'll spec a Mercury that matches the hull, since we're authorised on both brands). Lynn manages the parts counter for the consumables that come up over the season — fuel filters, lower-unit oil, prop hardware. Ron handles the parts orders for accessories like fish-finder mounting, anchor systems, and covers.

The Sportfisher is a pontoon Reyco specs out as lake-day-and-overnight configuration. Casey, Aaron, or Kory on the sales floor can walk you through trim options, powering decisions, and delivery timeline. Drop the boat off with the part and we'll get you back on the water.

Reyco runs as an authorised dealer for the brands we carry because the warranty backing and the in-house service are what make the long-term ownership work. We register the warranty before the unit leaves the lot. We book the break-in service before the customer asks. We carry the consumables on the parts counter so when something needs replacing, the customer walks out with the part the same day. That's the dealer-side value, and it's the reason customers in Northern Ontario keep buying from us across decades — not just one purchase. Casey, Aaron, and Kory on the sales floor can talk you through any of this; Lynn and Ron handle the parts side; Lee handles service. It's a small team that knows the customer, and we like it that way. We've watched generations of families buy boats here — fathers, sons, and now grandkids — and that's the kind of relationship a 60-plus-year dealership earns.

Name: 2026 Princecraft Jazz 17-ft Pontoon
Short description: 2026 Princecraft Jazz 17-ft pontoon — Reyco is an authorised Princecraft dealer in Sault Ste. Marie. Available off the lot at White Oak Drive. Talk to Casey about fit on your lake.
Description:
The 2026 Princecraft Jazz 17-ft pontoon is the kind of boat the cottage country around the Trans-Canada cottage country runs on summer weekends — built for relaxed cruising, family swims, sundown trolling, and the back-deck dinner that follows. Princecraft is a Canadian-built brand, and the Jazz sits in the part of the lineup we move most often through Reyco.

Casey carries Princecraft because the Canadian build matters in the Trans-Canada cottage country. The aluminum construction, the fit-and-finish on the deck and rails, and the Princecraft after-sales network are all reasons we've stocked the brand longer than most lineups on the floor. We're an authorised Princecraft dealer — the boat is registered, rigged, and water-tested through our service department before it leaves the lot.

Lee in the service department handles the rigging and outboard fit (we'll spec a Mercury that matches the hull, since we're authorised on both brands). Lynn manages the parts counter for the consumables that come up over the season — fuel filters, lower-unit oil, prop hardware. Ron handles the parts orders for accessories like fish-finder mounting, anchor systems, and covers.

The Jazz is a pontoon Reyco specs out as family-cruise configuration. Casey, Aaron, or Kory on the sales floor can walk you through trim options, powering decisions, and delivery timeline. Drop into 11 White Oak Drive East to see one on the floor.

Reyco's been on White Oak Drive for more than 60 years. The dealership has grown across boats, motors, and lawn-and-snow equipment, but the core of what we do hasn't changed — we sell what we'd put on our own gear, we service what we sell on our own bench, and we back the warranty paperwork in-house so the customer doesn't have to chase it. That's the part of an authorised-dealer purchase that doesn't show up on the price tag, and it's the reason most customers in Sault Ste. Marie don't think twice about where they go for marine and outdoor power equipment. We've earned that trust one boat, one motor, and one season at a time. The relationship is meant to run for years — not just the duration of a sale. When the unit needs attention, you stop in or you call, and someone on the team who knows you answers. That's the dealer side that customers tell us they value most.

Name: Echo CS-590 20-inch Chainsaw
Short description: Echo CS-590 20-inch Chainsaw — 20-inch bar — built for the customer who runs the saw, not just owns it. Authorised Echo dealer service from Damian in the service department.
Description:
The Echo CS-590 20-inch Chainsaw is the chainsaw we sell to the rural property holder clearing windfall every spring. It's the saw Aaron recommends when somebody walks in tired of replacing a box-store saw every two seasons.

Echo's professional-grade saws are over-built for homeowner use, which is why they end up in the shed for fifteen years instead of three. That over-engineering shows up on cold mornings — the Echo CS-590 20-inch Chainsaw starts cleanly when the box-store saws are still being yanked on. Northern Ontario winters are hard on small engines, and Echo's tolerance for cold-start abuse is one of the things the customer who's tired of the box-store saw lasting two seasons comes back for. Aaron sees these on the bench in October when everybody realises at the same time that wood's getting cut and they need a saw — get yours in before the rush.

Reyco is an authorised Echo dealer — warranty work, chain sharpening, parts, and routine service all run through our shop. Damian in the service department (small engines, lawn, snow, Echo) has been on these tools for years; Ron at the parts counter has the Echo-line consumables sorted before you finish asking. Most chainsaws don't get serviced when they should — bring yours in for a chain sharpening once a season and Damian will look it over while it's on the bench. Air filter, fuel filter, spark plug, bar oil — all wear items, all stocked at the parts counter.

For the customer who's tired of the box-store saw lasting two seasons, the Echo CS-590 20-inch Chainsaw pays for itself across two seasons of cuts versus a cheaper saw that needs replacing. Stop by the showroom at 11 White Oak Drive East to see it on the floor, or call 705-253-7828 if you'd rather talk through whether it's the right fit.

Name: Echo PB-580T Backpack Blower
Short description: Echo PB-580T Backpack Blower — Echo's answer for October leaf cleanup across a real Northern Ontario lot. Authorised dealer service from Damian at Reyco.
Description:
Echo's Echo PB-580T Backpack Blower is a tool you wear, not just hold — and the retiree who handles his own yard end-to-end feels the difference after the first hour. It's also one of the more reliable cold-start blowers in the line, which matters across the mixed cottage-and-rural lawns we get up here.

Backpack ergonomics matter more than spec sheets. The Echo PB-580T Backpack Blower is built so you can run it for the full leaf job without feeling it the next day. Echo's blowers run on a 50:1 mix and tolerate the cold-start abuse that Northern Ontario mornings throw at them. Air volume (CFM) does the work; air speed (MPH) gets the leaves moving — the Echo PB-580T Backpack Blower is balanced for both, which is why it shows up on so many landscaping trucks across the mixed cottage-and-rural lawns we get up here.

Reyco is an authorised Echo dealer — warranty work, chain sharpening, parts, and routine service all run through our shop. Damian in the service department (small engines, lawn, snow, Echo) has been on these tools for years; Ron at the parts counter has the Echo-line consumables sorted before you finish asking. Routine service on a blower is mostly air filter, spark plug, and an annual carb tune — Lynn and Ron keep the wear parts at the counter, Damian handles the carb work in-shop. We see these come back on schedule, not on warranty calls. Bring it in at the end of October once leaf season's wrapped and we'll get it set for spring.

For the year-round property caretaker, the Echo PB-580T Backpack Blower is the blower size that fits the property without overshooting. Echo's blower line is one of the few where the spec sheet matches the real-world performance — and Aaron will steer you to the right size for your lot rather than the bigger unit. Reyco's been on White Oak Drive East for more than 60 years. Stop by, talk to a tech, see the unit on the floor.

Name: 2026 Toro Z Master Zero-Turn Mower (77283)
Short description: Toro Z Master Zero-Turn Mower (77283) — Sized for the lawn in half the time for the half-acre-to-one-acre customer. Authorised Toro dealer service at Reyco.
Description:
When somebody asks Aaron 'what zero-turn,' the answer depends on the lot — for the weekend mower who wants the job done before noon, this is usually the answer. Toro's Z Master platform sits at the size and tier where most residential customers actually land.

Out across the half-acre lots in town, where most cottage and rural lots are obstacle courses (trees, septic mounds, gardens, gravel pads), the zero-turn pivot is what saves the time. The Toro Z Master Zero-Turn Mower (77283) sits in the sweet spot for a half-acre to a full acre — anything smaller and the deck size is overkill; anything bigger and you'd want a commercial unit. The deck pulls cleanly through long grass without bogging, which matters when the rain runs the grass three days past mowing day. Aaron points the customers who'd grown up on tractors toward this one when the lot makes the case for it — and most aren't sorry about the switch.

Reyco is an authorised Toro dealer — warranty work and platform-specific service come through our shop. Aaron runs the service side as Service Manager; Lynn manages the parts counter so wear items are usually a same-day pickup. That matters more on a zero-turn than on most platforms — the hydrostatic drive units, the spindle bearings, the deck mounts are all wear items that benefit from being looked at by a tech who knows the line. We see Toro zero-turns come back on schedule for routine deck-belt and spindle service around year four, which is normal. Less drama, more uptime.

For the retiree who finally bought the equipment he always wanted, the Toro Z Master Zero-Turn Mower (77283) is the upgrade that changes the relationship to the lawn — Saturdays back, cleaner cut, less time on the seat. Stop by the showroom at 11 White Oak Drive East to see it on the floor, or call 705-253-7828 if you'd rather talk through whether it's the right fit.

Name: 2026 Cub Cadet XT2 LX42 42-inch Riding Mower
Short description: Cub Cadet XT2 LX42 42-inch Riding Mower — The 42-inch deck handles a real Northern Ontario lawn. Authorised Cub Cadet dealer service from Aaron and Damian at White Oak Drive.
Description:
Every spring at Reyco we move a stack of Cub Cadet XT2 LX42 42-inch Riding Mowers off the floor — usually to the cottage owner with a one-acre lakefront lot. Cub Cadet's XT2 platform is the one Aaron recommends when somebody walks in describing a real lawn (not just a city lot) and asks for a unit that'll start every Saturday for the next ten years.

The XT2 platform is Cub Cadet's ride-on done right: hydrostatic drive (push the pedal, it goes), a comfortable seat for the long pass, and an electric PTO so engaging the deck is one button instead of a pull. For the customer mowing a half-acre Sault property up through a full lakefront acre, the geometry works — three or four passes per side and you're done. Up here, where the mowing window runs from late May to early October, you want a unit you can trust to start every Saturday — and that's where Cub Cadet's residential line earns its money. Aaron's been managing the service side here long enough to tell you which tier of the lineup actually pays off and which one's just hood badges — drop in and he'll walk you through it.

Reyco is an authorised Cub Cadet dealer — warranty work happens here in Sault Ste. Marie, no shipping the unit elsewhere. Aaron oversees the service side as Co-Owner and Service Manager; Damian handles the lawn-and-snow bench end to end; Lynn manages the parts counter. For the retiree who finally bought the equipment he always wanted, the trade-off pencils out: a unit that'll hold up across a decade of weekend mowing on the mixed cottage-and-rural lawns we get up here, with parts and service running through one shop instead of three. That's the part of the buy-here pitch that matters past year three — when the spindle starts to clunk or the deck belt walks, it's a half-hour conversation with Damian, not a six-week parts order.

Cub Cadet has been part of Reyco's lawn line since the early days, and we've serviced enough of these to know what holds up. Call 705-253-7828 to talk it through, or visit the showroom at 11 White Oak Drive East. We'll make sure the unit fits the lot before you leave the lot.

Name: 2026 Toro 326 50-inch Zero-Turn Mower (77501)
Short description: Toro 326 50-inch Zero-Turn Mower (77501) — The 50-inch deck handles the lawn in half the time for the half-acre-to-one-acre customer. Authorised Toro dealer service at Reyco.
Description:
The Toro 326 50-inch Zero-Turn Mower (77501) earns its keep across Northern Ontario when you've got more grass than time. Toro's TimeCutter platform was designed for residential customers who want to cut commercial-style — and the geometry works once you get used to it.

Out across Northern Ontario, where most cottage and rural lots are obstacle courses (trees, septic mounds, gardens, gravel pads), the zero-turn pivot is what saves the time. The Toro 326 50-inch Zero-Turn Mower (77501) sits in the sweet spot for a half-acre to a full acre — anything smaller and the deck size is overkill; anything bigger and you'd want a commercial unit. The 50-inch deck pulls cleanly through long grass without bogging, which matters when the rain runs the grass three days past mowing day. Aaron points the customers who'd grown up on tractors toward this one when the lot makes the case for it — and most aren't sorry about the switch.

Reyco is an authorised Toro dealer — warranty work and platform-specific service come through our shop. Aaron runs the service side as Service Manager; Lynn manages the parts counter so wear items are usually a same-day pickup. That matters more on a zero-turn than on most platforms — the hydrostatic drive units, the spindle bearings, the deck mounts are all wear items that benefit from being looked at by a tech who knows the line. We see Toro zero-turns come back on schedule for routine deck-belt and spindle service around year four, which is normal. Less drama, more uptime.

For the cottager prepping a place for renters every weekend, the Toro 326 50-inch Zero-Turn Mower (77501) is the upgrade that changes the relationship to the lawn — Saturdays back, cleaner cut, less time on the seat. Drop in at 11 White Oak Drive East to see it on the floor. Lynn manages the parts counter and Ron can pull most wear items same-day.

Name: 2026 Cub Cadet XT1 LT46 46-inch Riding Mower IntelliPOWER
Short description: Cub Cadet XT1 LT46 46-inch Riding Mower IntelliPOWER — The 46-inch deck handles a real Northern Ontario lawn. Authorised Cub Cadet dealer service from Aaron and Damian at White Oak Drive.
Description:
Every spring at Reyco we move a stack of Cub Cadet XT1 LT46 46-inch Riding Mower IntelliPOWERs off the floor — usually to the property caretaker juggling four or five lakefront places. Cub Cadet's XT1 platform is the one Aaron recommends when somebody walks in describing a real lawn (not just a city lot) and asks for a unit that'll start every Saturday for the next ten years.

The XT1 platform is Cub Cadet's ride-on done right: hydrostatic drive (push the pedal, it goes), a comfortable seat for the long pass, and an electric PTO so engaging the deck is one button instead of a pull. For the customer mowing a half-acre Sault property up through a full lakefront acre, the geometry works — three or four passes per side and you're done. Up here, where the mowing window runs from late May to early October, you want a unit you can trust to start every Saturday — and that's where Cub Cadet's residential line earns its money. Aaron's been managing the service side here long enough to tell you which tier of the lineup actually pays off and which one's just hood badges — drop in and he'll walk you through it.

Reyco is an authorised Cub Cadet dealer — warranty work happens here in Sault Ste. Marie, no shipping the unit elsewhere. Aaron oversees the service side as Co-Owner and Service Manager; Damian handles the lawn-and-snow bench end to end; Lynn manages the parts counter. For the cottage owner with a one-acre lakefront lot, the trade-off pencils out: a unit that'll hold up across a decade of weekend mowing on the cottage country lots, with parts and service running through one shop instead of three. That's the part of the buy-here pitch that matters past year three — when the spindle starts to clunk or the deck belt walks, it's a half-hour conversation with Damian, not a six-week parts order.

Cub Cadet has been part of Reyco's lawn line since the early days, and we've serviced enough of these to know what holds up. Call 705-253-7828 or stop in at 11 White Oak Drive East — Aaron or Damian will walk you through what fits your property.

Name: 2026 Toro TimeCutter 54-inch Zero-Turn Mower (77503)
Short description: Toro TimeCutter 54-inch Zero-Turn Mower (77503) — The 54-inch deck handles the lawn in half the time for the half-acre-to-one-acre customer. Authorised Toro dealer service at Reyco.
Description:
If you've got a property where straight rows and tight pivots both matter, the Toro TimeCutter 54-inch Zero-Turn Mower (77503) is built for it. Zero-turns trade tractor steering for raw productivity — once you're comfortable on one, you'll cut the same lawn in half the time, with cleaner trim around obstacles.

Out across the cottage roads east of town, where most cottage and rural lots are obstacle courses (trees, septic mounds, gardens, gravel pads), the zero-turn pivot is what saves the time. The Toro TimeCutter 54-inch Zero-Turn Mower (77503) sits in the sweet spot for a half-acre to a full acre — anything smaller and the deck size is overkill; anything bigger and you'd want a commercial unit. The 54-inch deck pulls cleanly through long grass without bogging, which matters when the rain runs the grass three days past mowing day. Aaron points the customers who'd grown up on tractors toward this one when the lot makes the case for it — and most aren't sorry about the switch.

Reyco is an authorised Toro dealer — warranty work and platform-specific service come through our shop. Aaron runs the service side as Service Manager; Lynn manages the parts counter so wear items are usually a same-day pickup. That matters more on a zero-turn than on most platforms — the hydrostatic drive units, the spindle bearings, the deck mounts are all wear items that benefit from being looked at by a tech who knows the line. We see Toro zero-turns come back on schedule for routine deck-belt and spindle service around year four, which is normal. Less drama, more uptime.

For the in-town buyer with a flat lot and a tight Saturday schedule, the Toro TimeCutter 54-inch Zero-Turn Mower (77503) is the upgrade that changes the relationship to the lawn — Saturdays back, cleaner cut, less time on the seat. If you've got questions about fit, call 705-253-7828. If you want to see the unit, we're at 11 White Oak Drive East in Sault Ste. Marie.

Name: 2026 Cub Cadet XT1 LT42 42-inch Riding Mower (13A6A9TS596)
Short description: Cub Cadet XT1 LT42 42-inch Riding Mower (13A6A9TS596) — The 42-inch deck handles a real Northern Ontario lawn. Authorised Cub Cadet dealer service from Aaron and Damian at White Oak Drive.
Description:
For the homeowner with a tight half-acre suburban lot, the Cub Cadet XT1 LT42 42-inch Riding Mower (13A6A9TS596) hits the sweet spot. Cub Cadet's XT1 platform is built around the customer who wants to finish the lawn before the mosquitoes come up — and the 42-inch deck does exactly that. We see this size move off the floor every spring at Reyco. It's the unit Aaron points cottage country buyers toward when they describe their property and ask 'what fits?'

The XT1 platform is Cub Cadet's ride-on done right: hydrostatic drive (push the pedal, it goes), a comfortable seat for the long pass, and an electric PTO so engaging the deck is one button instead of a pull. For the customer mowing a half-acre Sault property up through a full lakefront acre, the geometry works — three or four passes per side and you're done. Up here, where the mowing window runs from late May to early October, you want a unit you can trust to start every Saturday — and that's where Cub Cadet's residential line earns its money. Aaron's been managing the service side here long enough to tell you which tier of the lineup actually pays off and which one's just hood badges — drop in and he'll walk you through it.

Reyco is an authorised Cub Cadet dealer — warranty work happens here in Sault Ste. Marie, no shipping the unit elsewhere. Aaron oversees the service side as Co-Owner and Service Manager; Damian handles the lawn-and-snow bench end to end; Lynn manages the parts counter. For the rural property holder with the back forty to manage, the trade-off pencils out: a unit that'll hold up across a decade of weekend mowing on the cottage roads east of town, with parts and service running through one shop instead of three. That's the part of the buy-here pitch that matters past year three — when the spindle starts to clunk or the deck belt walks, it's a half-hour conversation with Damian, not a six-week parts order.

Cub Cadet has been part of Reyco's lawn line since the early days, and we've serviced enough of these to know what holds up. Talk to Damian in the service department (small engines, lawn, snow, Echo) — he'll tell you what holds up and what to expect from the platform. 705-253-7828 or stop in.

Name: 2026 Cub Cadet XT1 LT46 46-inch Riding Mower (13B6A9TTA10)
Short description: Cub Cadet XT1 LT46 46-inch Riding Mower (13B6A9TTA10) — The 46-inch deck handles a real Northern Ontario lawn. Authorised Cub Cadet dealer service from Aaron and Damian at White Oak Drive.
Description:
The Cub Cadet XT1 LT46 46-inch Riding Mower (13B6A9TTA10) is the tractor we sell to the in-town buyer with a flat lot and a tight Saturday schedule. Aaron's seen this platform run reliably across Algoma District for years — it's the unit that gets pulled out the first warm Saturday in May and run hard through October. Cub Cadet's XT1 platform sits in the sweet spot for the in-between lot — too much lawn for a push mower, not enough to justify a zero-turn.

The XT1 platform is Cub Cadet's ride-on done right: hydrostatic drive (push the pedal, it goes), a comfortable seat for the long pass, and an electric PTO so engaging the deck is one button instead of a pull. For the customer mowing a half-acre Sault property up through a full lakefront acre, the geometry works — three or four passes per side and you're done. Up here, where the mowing window runs from late May to early October, you want a unit you can trust to start every Saturday — and that's where Cub Cadet's residential line earns its money. Aaron's been managing the service side here long enough to tell you which tier of the lineup actually pays off and which one's just hood badges — drop in and he'll walk you through it.

Reyco is an authorised Cub Cadet dealer — warranty work happens here in Sault Ste. Marie, no shipping the unit elsewhere. Aaron oversees the service side as Co-Owner and Service Manager; Damian handles the lawn-and-snow bench end to end; Lynn manages the parts counter. For the rural property holder with the back forty to manage, the trade-off pencils out: a unit that'll hold up across a decade of weekend mowing on the Sault, with parts and service running through one shop instead of three. That's the part of the buy-here pitch that matters past year three — when the spindle starts to clunk or the deck belt walks, it's a half-hour conversation with Damian, not a six-week parts order.

Cub Cadet has been part of Reyco's lawn line since the early days, and we've serviced enough of these to know what holds up. If you've got questions about fit, call 705-253-7828. If you want to see the unit, we're at 11 White Oak Drive East in Sault Ste. Marie.

Name: 2025 Toro 77507 Zero-Turn Mower
Short description: Toro 77507 Zero-Turn Mower — Sized for the lawn in half the time for the half-acre-to-one-acre customer. Authorised Toro dealer service at Reyco.
Description:
If you've got a property where straight rows and tight pivots both matter, the Toro 77507 Zero-Turn Mower is built for it. Zero-turns trade tractor steering for raw productivity — once you're comfortable on one, you'll cut the same lawn in half the time, with cleaner trim around obstacles.

Out across the Sault, where most cottage and rural lots are obstacle courses (trees, septic mounds, gardens, gravel pads), the zero-turn pivot is what saves the time. The Toro 77507 Zero-Turn Mower sits in the sweet spot for a half-acre to a full acre — anything smaller and the deck size is overkill; anything bigger and you'd want a commercial unit. The deck pulls cleanly through long grass without bogging, which matters when the rain runs the grass three days past mowing day. Aaron points the customers who'd grown up on tractors toward this one when the lot makes the case for it — and most aren't sorry about the switch.

Reyco is an authorised Toro dealer — warranty work and platform-specific service come through our shop. Aaron runs the service side as Service Manager; Lynn manages the parts counter so wear items are usually a same-day pickup. That matters more on a zero-turn than on most platforms — the hydrostatic drive units, the spindle bearings, the deck mounts are all wear items that benefit from being looked at by a tech who knows the line. We see Toro zero-turns come back on schedule for routine deck-belt and spindle service around year four, which is normal. Less drama, more uptime.

For the seasonal landscaper running multiple properties, the Toro 77507 Zero-Turn Mower is the upgrade that changes the relationship to the lawn — Saturdays back, cleaner cut, less time on the seat. Call 705-253-7828 or stop in at 11 White Oak Drive East — Aaron or Damian will walk you through what fits your property.

Name: 2026 Cub Cadet XT2 LX46 46-inch Riding Mower (13APA1TNA10)
Short description: Cub Cadet XT2 LX46 46-inch Riding Mower (13APA1TNA10) — The 46-inch deck handles a real Northern Ontario lawn. Authorised Cub Cadet dealer service from Aaron and Damian at White Oak Drive.
Description:
The Cub Cadet XT2 LX46 46-inch Riding Mower (13APA1TNA10) is the tractor we sell to the customer who treats the lawn like a project, not a chore. Aaron's seen this platform run reliably across Algoma District for years — it's the unit that gets pulled out the first warm Saturday in May and run hard through October. Cub Cadet's XT2 platform sits in the sweet spot for the in-between lot — too much lawn for a push mower, not enough to justify a zero-turn.

The XT2 platform is Cub Cadet's ride-on done right: hydrostatic drive (push the pedal, it goes), a comfortable seat for the long pass, and an electric PTO so engaging the deck is one button instead of a pull. For the customer mowing a half-acre Sault property up through a full lakefront acre, the geometry works — three or four passes per side and you're done. Up here, where the mowing window runs from late May to early October, you want a unit you can trust to start every Saturday — and that's where Cub Cadet's residential line earns its money. Aaron's been managing the service side here long enough to tell you which tier of the lineup actually pays off and which one's just hood badges — drop in and he'll walk you through it.

Reyco is an authorised Cub Cadet dealer — warranty work happens here in Sault Ste. Marie, no shipping the unit elsewhere. Aaron oversees the service side as Co-Owner and Service Manager; Damian handles the lawn-and-snow bench end to end; Lynn manages the parts counter. For the cottager prepping a place for renters every weekend, the trade-off pencils out: a unit that'll hold up across a decade of weekend mowing on the rural lots north of the city, with parts and service running through one shop instead of three. That's the part of the buy-here pitch that matters past year three — when the spindle starts to clunk or the deck belt walks, it's a half-hour conversation with Damian, not a six-week parts order.

Cub Cadet has been part of Reyco's lawn line since the early days, and we've serviced enough of these to know what holds up. Call 705-253-7828 or stop in at 11 White Oak Drive East — Aaron or Damian will walk you through what fits your property.

Name: 2026 Toro TurfMaster 30-inch Push Mower (22217)
Short description: Toro TurfMaster 30-inch Push Mower (22217) — practical push mower for the smaller lot. Authorised Toro dealer at White Oak Drive.
Description:
For the smaller lot — the front yard, the boulevard strip, the rental — the Toro TurfMaster 30-inch Push Mower (22217) is the one we recommend. Not every lawn calls for a tractor. the cottager prepping a place for renters every weekend is often better off with a push mower that lives in the shed eleven months a year and starts cleanly in May.

A push mower lives or dies on three things: cold-start reliability, handle ergonomics, and how easy it is to service. Toro's residential push line is built around all three. The Toro TurfMaster 30-inch Push Mower (22217) is the one Aaron sells most across Algoma District — to retirees, to renters, to the in-town buyer with a tight lot. It's a unit that doesn't get talked about much because it works — and that's exactly the recommendation you want from a parts-counter shop, not a sales-floor pitch.

Reyco is an authorised Toro dealer — warranty work and platform-specific service come through our shop. Aaron runs the service side as Service Manager; Lynn manages the parts counter so wear items are usually a same-day pickup. On a push mower the biggest service items are the air filter and the spark plug — Lynn keeps those at the parts counter, same-day pickup if you walk in. The blade we'll sharpen on the bench while you wait, if it's not too gnarled.

For the in-town buyer with a flat lot and a tight Saturday schedule who isn't ready for a tractor and doesn't want a cordless, the Toro TurfMaster 30-inch Push Mower (22217) is the right size and platform. Talk to Damian in the service department (small engines, lawn, snow, Echo) — he'll tell you what holds up and what to expect from the platform. 705-253-7828 or stop in. Aaron's bench read: the push mowers that hold up are the ones that get the spark plug changed every couple seasons and the deck scraped clean of grass clippings. Two five-minute jobs that double the service life.

Name: 2026 Toro TimeCutter 42-inch Zero-Turn Mower (77404)
Short description: Toro TimeCutter 42-inch Zero-Turn Mower (77404) — The 42-inch deck handles the lawn in half the time for the half-acre-to-one-acre customer. Authorised Toro dealer service at Reyco.
Description:
If you've got a property where straight rows and tight pivots both matter, the Toro TimeCutter 42-inch Zero-Turn Mower (77404) is built for it. Zero-turns trade tractor steering for raw productivity — once you're comfortable on one, you'll cut the same lawn in half the time, with cleaner trim around obstacles.

Out across the cottage country lots, where most cottage and rural lots are obstacle courses (trees, septic mounds, gardens, gravel pads), the zero-turn pivot is what saves the time. The Toro TimeCutter 42-inch Zero-Turn Mower (77404) sits in the sweet spot for a half-acre to a full acre — anything smaller and the deck size is overkill; anything bigger and you'd want a commercial unit. The 42-inch deck pulls cleanly through long grass without bogging, which matters when the rain runs the grass three days past mowing day. Aaron points the customers who'd grown up on tractors toward this one when the lot makes the case for it — and most aren't sorry about the switch.

Reyco is an authorised Toro dealer — warranty work and platform-specific service come through our shop. Aaron runs the service side as Service Manager; Lynn manages the parts counter so wear items are usually a same-day pickup. That matters more on a zero-turn than on most platforms — the hydrostatic drive units, the spindle bearings, the deck mounts are all wear items that benefit from being looked at by a tech who knows the line. We see Toro zero-turns come back on schedule for routine deck-belt and spindle service around year four, which is normal. Less drama, more uptime.

For the rural property holder with the back forty to manage, the Toro TimeCutter 42-inch Zero-Turn Mower (77404) is the upgrade that changes the relationship to the lawn — Saturdays back, cleaner cut, less time on the seat. Stop by the showroom at 11 White Oak Drive East to see it on the floor, or call 705-253-7828 if you'd rather talk through whether it's the right fit.

Name: 2025 Cub Cadet ZTS46 46-inch Zero-Turn Mower (17ARGBYNA10)
Short description: Cub Cadet ZTS46 46-inch Zero-Turn Mower (17ARGBYNA10) — The 46-inch deck handles a Northern Ontario lawn in half the time. Authorised Cub Cadet dealer service from Aaron and Damian at White Oak Drive.
Description:
When somebody asks Aaron 'what zero-turn,' the answer depends on the lot — for the in-town buyer with a flat lot and a tight Saturday schedule, this is usually the answer. Cub Cadet's ZTS4 platform sits at the size and tier where most residential customers actually land.

Out across the rural lots north of the city, where most cottage and rural lots are obstacle courses (trees, septic mounds, gardens, gravel pads), the zero-turn pivot is what saves the time. The Cub Cadet ZTS46 46-inch Zero-Turn Mower (17ARGBYNA10) sits in the sweet spot for a half-acre to a full acre — anything smaller and the deck size is overkill; anything bigger and you'd want a commercial unit. The 46-inch deck pulls cleanly through long grass without bogging, which matters when the rain runs the grass three days past mowing day. Aaron points the customers who'd grown up on tractors toward this one when the lot makes the case for it — and most aren't sorry about the switch.

Reyco is an authorised Cub Cadet dealer — warranty work happens here in Sault Ste. Marie, no shipping the unit elsewhere. Aaron oversees the service side as Co-Owner and Service Manager; Damian handles the lawn-and-snow bench end to end; Lynn manages the parts counter. That matters more on a zero-turn than on most platforms — the hydrostatic drive units, the spindle bearings, the deck mounts are all wear items that benefit from being looked at by a tech who knows the line. We see Cub Cadet zero-turns come back on schedule for routine deck-belt and spindle service around year four, which is normal. Less drama, more uptime.

For the rural property holder with the back forty to manage, the Cub Cadet ZTS46 46-inch Zero-Turn Mower (17ARGBYNA10) is the upgrade that changes the relationship to the lawn — Saturdays back, cleaner cut, less time on the seat. Reyco's been on White Oak Drive East for more than 60 years. Stop by, talk to a tech, see the unit on the floor.
